text.NRSV: For you were bought with a price; therefore glorify God in your body.
text.Rotherham: For ye have been bought with a price! Therefore glorify God in your body.
text.KJV: For ye are bought with a price: therefore glorify God in your body, and in your spirit, which are God’s.
text.NABRE: For you have been purchased at a price. Therefore glorify God in your body.
text.DRB: For you are bought with a great price. Glorify and bear God in your body.

text.NRSV: Now concerning the matters about which you wrote: “It is well for a man not to touch a woman.”
text.Rotherham: Now, concerning the things whereof ye wrote, it were, good, for a man, not to touch, a woman;
text.KJV: Now concerning the things whereof ye wrote unto me: It is good for a man not to touch a woman.
text.NABRE: III. Answers to the Corinthians’ Questions A. Marriage and Virginity Chapter 7 Advice to the Married. Now in regard to the matters about which you wrote: “It is a good thing for a man not to touch a woman,”
text.DRB: And concerning the things whereof you wrote to me: It is good for a man not to touch a woman.

text.NRSV: But because of cases of sexual immorality, each man should have his own wife and each woman her own husband.
text.Rotherham: But, on account of fornications, let, each man, have, his own wife, and, each woman, have, her own husband:
text.KJV: Nevertheless, to avoid fornication, let every man have his own wife, and let every woman have her own husband.
text.NABRE: but because of cases of immorality every man should have his own wife, and every woman her own husband.
text.DRB: But because of fornication let every man have his own wife, & let every woman have her own husband.

text.NRSV: The husband should give to his wife her conjugal rights, and likewise the wife to her husband.
text.Rotherham: Unto the wife, let the husband render what is her due, and, in like manner, the wife also, unto the husband,—
text.KJV: Let the husband render unto the wife due benevolence: and likewise also the wife unto the husband.
text.NABRE: The husband should fulfill his duty toward his wife, and likewise the wife toward her husband.
text.DRB: Let the husband render his debt to the wife: and the wife also in like manner to her husband.

text.NRSV: For the wife does not have authority over her own body, but the husband does; likewise the husband does not have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
text.Rotherham: The wife, over her own body, hath not authority, but the husband, and, in like manner, the husband also, over his own body, hath not authority, but the wife.
text.KJV: The wife hath not power of her own body, but the husband: and likewise also the husband hath not power of his own body, but the wife.
text.NABRE: A wife does not have authority over her own body, but rather her husband, and similarly a husband does not have authority over his own body, but rather his wife.
text.DRB: The woman hath not power of her own body: but her husband. And in like manner the man also hath not power of his own body; but the woman.

text.NRSV: Do not deprive one another, except perhaps by agreement for a set time, to devote yourselves to prayer, and then come together again, so that Satan may not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
text.Rotherham: Be not depriving one another—unless perhaps by consent for a season, that ye may have leisure for prayer, and, again, may be together,—lest Satan be tempting you by reason of your want of self-control.
text.KJV: Defraud ye not one the other, except it be with consent for a time, that ye may give yourselves to fasting and prayer; and come together again, that Satan tempt you not for your incontinency.
text.NABRE: Do not deprive each other, except perhaps by mutual consent for a time, to be free for prayer, but then return to one another, so that Satan may not tempt you through your lack of self-control.
text.DRB: Defraud not one another, except perhaps by consent for a time, that you may give yourself to prayer: and return again together, lest Satan tempt you for your incontinency.

text.NRSV: This I say by way of concession, not of command.
text.Rotherham: This, however I am saying, by way of concession, not of injunction;
text.KJV: But I speak this by permission, and not of commandment.
text.NABRE: This I say by way of concession, however, not as a command.
text.DRB: But I say this by indulgence, not by commandment.

text.NRSV: I wish that all were as I myself am. But each has a particular gift from God, one of one kind and one of another.
text.Rotherham: Besides, I desire all men to be, even as myself,—but, each one, hath his, personal, gift from God, one, after this manner, and, another, after that.
text.KJV: For I would that all men were even as I myself. But every man hath his proper gift of God, one after this manner, and another after that.
text.NABRE: Indeed, I wish everyone to be as I am, but each has a particular gift from God, one of one kind and one of another.
text.DRB: For I would all men to be as myself: but every one hath a proper gift of God; one so, and another so.

text.NRSV: To the unmarried and the widows I say that it is well for them to remain unmarried as I am.
text.Rotherham: But I say, to the unmarried, and to the widows, good, were it for them, that they should abide, even as I;
text.KJV: I say therefore to the unmarried and widows, It is good for them if they abide even as I.
text.NABRE: Now to the unmarried and to widows I say: it is a good thing for them to remain as they are, as I do,
text.DRB: But I say to the unmarried and to widows: It is good for them if they so abide even as I also.

text.NRSV: But if they are not practicing self-control, they should marry. For it is better to marry than to be aflame with passion.
text.Rotherham: But, if they have not self-control, let them marry, for, better, is it, to marry than to burn.
text.KJV: But if they cannot contain, let them marry: for it is better to marry than to burn.
text.NABRE: but if they cannot exercise self-control they should marry, for it is better to marry than to be on fire.
text.DRB: But if they do not contain themselves, let them marry. For it is better to marry than to be burnt.

text.NRSV: To the married I give this command—not I but the Lord—that the wife should not separate from her husband
text.Rotherham: To the married, however, I give charge—not, I, but the Lord,—that, a wife, from her husband, do not depart,—
text.KJV: And unto the married I command, yet not I, but the Lord, Let not the wife depart from her husband:
text.NABRE: To the married, however, I give this instruction (not I, but the Lord): A wife should not separate from her husband
text.DRB: But to them that be joined in matrimony, not I give commandment, but our Lord, that the wife depart not from her husband:

text.NRSV: (but if she does separate, let her remain unmarried or else be reconciled to her husband), and that the husband should not divorce his wife.
text.Rotherham: But, if she should even depart, let her remain unmarried, or, to her husband, be reconciled; and let not, a husband, leave, his wife.
text.KJV: But and if she depart, let her remain unmarried, or be reconciled to her husband: and let not the husband put away his wife.
text.NABRE: —and if she does separate she must either remain single or become reconciled to her husband—and a husband should not divorce his wife.
text.DRB: and if she depart, to remain unmarried, or to be reconciled to her husband. And let not the husband put away his wife.

text.NRSV: To the rest I say—I and not the Lord—that if any believer has a wife who is an unbeliever, and she consents to live with him, he should not divorce her.
text.Rotherham: But, unto the rest, say, I—not the Lord,—if, any brother, hath, a wife that believeth not, and, she, is well pleased to dwell with him, let him not leave her;
text.KJV: But to the rest speak I, not the Lord: If any brother hath a wife that believeth not, and she be pleased to dwell with him, let him not put her away.
text.NABRE: To the rest I say (not the Lord): if any brother has a wife who is an unbeliever, and she is willing to go on living with him, he should not divorce her;
text.DRB: For to the rest, I say, not our Lord. If any brother have a wife an infidel, and she consent to dwell with him; let him not put her away.

text.NRSV: And if any woman has a husband who is an unbeliever, and he consents to live with her, she should not divorce him.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman who hath a husband that believeth not, and, he, is well pleased to dwell with her, let her not leave her husband;—
text.KJV: And the woman which hath an husband that believeth not, and if he be pleased to dwell with her, let her not leave him.
text.NABRE: and if any woman has a husband who is an unbeliever, and he is willing to go on living with her, she should not divorce her husband.
text.DRB: And if any woman have a husband an infidel, and he consent to dwell with her; let her not put away her husband.

text.NRSV: For the unbelieving husband is made holy through his wife, and the unbelieving wife is made holy through her husband. Otherwise, your children would be unclean, but as it is, they are holy.
text.Rotherham: For the husband that believeth not is hallowed in the wife, and the wife that believeth not is hallowed in the brother: else were, your children, impure, but, now, are they, pure.
text.KJV: For the unbelieving husband is sanctified by the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified by the husband: else were your children unclean; but now are they holy.
text.NABRE: For the unbelieving husband is made holy through his wife, and the unbelieving wife is made holy through the brother. Otherwise your children would be unclean, whereas in fact they are holy.
text.DRB: For the man an infidel is sanctified by the faithful woman; and the woman an infidel is sanctified by the faithful husband: otherwise your children should be unclean; but now they are holy.

text.NRSV: But if the unbelieving partner separates, let it be so; in such a case the brother or sister is not bound. It is to peace that God has called you.
text.Rotherham: But, if, the unbelieving, departeth, let him depart: the brother or the sister hath not come into bondage, in such cases, but, in peace, hath God called us.
text.KJV: But if the unbelieving depart, let him depart. A brother or a sister is not under bondage in such cases: but God hath called us to peace.
text.NABRE: If the unbeliever separates, however, let him separate. The brother or sister is not bound in such cases; God has called you to peace.
text.DRB: But if the infidel depart, let him depart. For the brother or sister is not subject to servitude in such. But in peace hath God called us.

text.NRSV: Wife, for all you know, you might save your husband. Husband, for all you know, you might save your wife.
text.Rotherham: For how knowest thou, O woman, whether, thy husband, thou shalt save? Or how knowest thou, O man, whether, thy wife, thou shalt save?
text.KJV: For what knowest thou, O wife, whether thou shalt save thy husband? or how knowest thou, O man, whether thou shalt save thy wife?
text.NABRE: For how do you know, wife, whether you will save your husband; or how do you know, husband, whether you will save your wife? The Life That the Lord Has Assigned.
text.DRB: For how knowest thou woman, if thou shalt save thy husband? or how knowest thou man, if thou shalt save the woman?

text.NRSV: However that may be, let each of you lead the life that the Lord has assigned, to which God has called you. This is my rule in all the churches.
text.Rotherham: If not—as the Lord hath distributed—unto each one, as God hath called, each one, so, let him be walking;—and, so, in all the assemblies, I ordain.
text.KJV: But as God hath distributed to every man, as the Lord hath called every one, so let him walk. And so ordain I in all churches.
text.NABRE: Only, everyone should live as the Lord has assigned, just as God called each one. I give this order in all the churches.
text.DRB: But to every one as our Lord hath divided, as God hath called every one, so let him walk, and as in all Churches I teach.

text.NRSV: Was anyone at the time of his call already circumcised? Let him not seek to remove the marks of circumcision. Was anyone at the time of his call uncircumcised? Let him not seek circumcision.
text.Rotherham: After being circumcised, was any called? let him not become uncircumcised; in uncircumcision, hath any been called? let him not be circumcised:
text.KJV: Is any man called being circumcised? let him not become uncircumcised. Is any called in uncircumcision? let him not be circumcised.
text.NABRE: Was someone called after he had been circumcised? He should not try to undo his circumcision. Was an uncircumcised person called? He should not be circumcised.
text.DRB: Is any man called being circumcised? Let him not procure prepuce. Is any man called in prepuce? Let him not be circumcised.

text.NRSV: Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing; but obeying the commandments of God is everything.
text.Rotherham: The circumcision, is, nothing, and, the uncircumcision, is, nothing,—but keeping the commandments of God.
text.KJV: Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing, but the keeping of the commandments of God.
text.NABRE: Circumcision means nothing, and uncircumcision means nothing; what matters is keeping God’s commandments.
text.DRB: circumcision is nothing, and prepuce is nothing: but the observation of the commandments of God.

text.NRSV: Let each of you remain in the condition in which you were called.
text.Rotherham: Each one, in the calling wherein he was called, in the same, let him abide:
text.KJV: Let every man abide in the same calling wherein he was called.
text.NABRE: Everyone should remain in the state in which he was called.
text.DRB: Every one in the vocation that he was called, in it let him abide.

text.NRSV: Were you a slave when called? Do not be concerned about it. Even if you can gain your freedom, make use of your present condition now more than ever.
text.Rotherham: A bond-servant, wast thou called? let it not cause thee concern; but, if thou canst even become, free, rather use it.
text.KJV: Art thou called being a servant? care not for it: but if thou mayest be made free, use it rather.
text.NABRE: Were you a slave when you were called? Do not be concerned but, even if you can gain your freedom, make the most of it.
text.DRB: Wast thou called being a bondman? care not for it: but and if thou canst be made free, use it rather.

text.NRSV: For whoever was called in the Lord as a slave is a freed person belonging to the Lord, just as whoever was free when called is a slave of Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, he who in the Lord was called, being a bond-servant, is, a freed-man of the Lord: in like manner, he that was called being, a freeman, is Christ’s bond-servant:—
text.KJV: For he that is called in the Lord, being a servant, is the Lord’s freeman: likewise also he that is called, being free, is Christ’s servant.
text.NABRE: For the slave called in the Lord is a freed person in the Lord, just as the free person who has been called is a slave of Christ.
text.DRB: For he that in our Lord is called, being a bondman, is the franchised of our Lord. Likewise he that is called, being free, is the bondman of Christ.

text.NRSV: You were bought with a price; do not become slaves of human beings.
text.Rotherham: With a price, have ye been bought,—do not become bond-servants of men:
text.KJV: Ye are bought with a price; be not ye the servants of men.
text.NABRE: You have been purchased at a price. Do not become slaves to human beings.
text.DRB: You were bought with price, be not made the bondmen of men.

text.NRSV: In whatever condition you were called, brothers and sisters, there remain with God.
text.Rotherham: Each one, wherein he was called, brethren, in the same, let him abide with God.
text.KJV: Brethren, let every man, wherein he is called, therein abide with God.
text.NABRE: Brothers, everyone should continue before God in the state in which he was called.
text.DRB: Every brother wherein he was called, in that let him abide before God.

text.NRSV: Now concerning virgins, I have no command of the Lord, but I give my opinion as one who by the Lord’s mercy is trustworthy.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning them who are virgin, injunction of the Lord, have I none; yet, a judgment, do I give, as one who hath obtained mercy from the Lord to be, faithful:
text.KJV: Now concerning virgins I have no commandment of the Lord: yet I give my judgment, as one that hath obtained mercy of the Lord to be faithful.
text.NABRE: Advice to Virgins and Widows. Now in regard to virgins I have no commandment from the Lord, but I give my opinion as one who by the Lord’s mercy is trustworthy.
text.DRB: And as concerning virgins, a commandment of our Lord I have not: but counsel I give, as having obtained mercy of our Lord to be faithful.

text.NRSV: I think that, in view of the impending crisis, it is well for you to remain as you are.
text.Rotherham: I consider this, then, to be, good, in the circumstances, by reason of the existing distress,—that it is, good for a man, so, to be:
text.KJV: I suppose therefore that this is good for the present distress, I say, that it is good for a man so to be.
text.NABRE: So this is what I think best because of the present distress: that it is a good thing for a person to remain as he is.
text.DRB: Ithink therefore that this is good for the present necessity, because it is good for a man so to be.

text.NRSV: Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek to be free. Are you free from a wife? Do not seek a wife.
text.Rotherham: Hast thou become bound to a wife? do not seek to be loosed; hast thou become loosed from a wife? do not seek a wife.
text.KJV: Art thou bound unto a wife? seek not to be loosed. Art thou loosed from a wife? seek not a wife.
text.NABRE: Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek a separation. Are you free of a wife? Then do not look for a wife.
text.DRB: Art thou tied to a wife? seek not to be loosed. Art thou loose from a wife? seek not a wife.

text.NRSV: But if you marry, you do not sin, and if a virgin marries, she does not sin. Yet those who marry will experience distress in this life, and I would spare you that.
text.Rotherham: If, however, thou shouldst even marry, thou hast not sinned; and, if one who is virgin should marry, that one hath not sinned;—but, tribulation of the flesh, shall, such, have:—howbeit, I, spare you.
text.KJV: But and if thou marry, thou hast not sinned; and if a virgin marry, she hath not sinned. Nevertheless such shall have trouble in the flesh: but I spare you.
text.NABRE: If you marry, however, you do not sin, nor does an unmarried woman sin if she marries; but such people will experience affliction in their earthly life, and I would like to spare you that.
text.DRB: But if thou take a wife, thou hast not sinned. And if a virgin marry, she hath not sinned. Nevertheless tribulation of the flesh shall such have. But I spare you.

text.NRSV: I mean, brothers and sisters, the appointed time has grown short; from now on, let even those who have wives be as though they had none,
text.Rotherham: But, this, I say—the opportunity is, contracted for what remaineth—in order that, they who have wives, may be, as though they had none,
text.KJV: But this I say, brethren, the time is short: it remaineth, that both they that have wives be as though they had none;
text.NABRE: I tell you, brothers, the time is running out. From now on, let those having wives act as not having them,
text.DRB: This therefore I say, brethren; the time is short, it remaineth, that they also which have wives, be as though they had not;

text.NRSV: and those who mourn as though they were not mourning, and those who rejoice as though they were not rejoicing, and those who buy as though they had no possessions,
text.Rotherham: And, they who weep, as though they wept not, and, they who rejoice, as though they rejoiced not, and, they who buy, as though they possessed not,
text.KJV: And they that weep, as though they wept not; and they that rejoice, as though they rejoiced not; and they that buy, as though they possessed not;
text.NABRE: those weeping as not weeping, those rejoicing as not rejoicing, those buying as not owning,
text.DRB: and they that weep, as though they wept not; and they that rejoice, as though they rejoiced not; and they that buy, as though they possessed not;

text.NRSV: and those who deal with the world as though they had no dealings with it. For the present form of this world is passing away.
text.Rotherham: And, they who use the world, as though they used it not to the full,—for the fashion of this world passeth away;
text.KJV: And they that use this world, as not abusing it: for the fashion of this world passeth away.
text.NABRE: those using the world as not using it fully. For the world in its present form is passing away.
text.DRB: and they that use this world, as though they used it not. For the figure of this world passeth away.

text.NRSV: I want you to be free from anxieties. The unmarried man is anxious about the affairs of the Lord, how to please the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And I desire you to be, without anxiety:—the unmarried man, is anxious for the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord;
text.KJV: But I would have you without carefulness. He that is unmarried careth for the things that belong to the Lord, how he may please the Lord:
text.NABRE: I should like you to be free of anxieties. An unmarried man is anxious about the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord.
text.DRB: But I would have you to be without carefulness. He that is without a wife, is careful for the things that pertain to our Lord, how he may please God.

text.NRSV: but the married man is anxious about the affairs of the world, how to please his wife,
text.Rotherham: But, he that hath married, is anxious for the things of the world, how may please his wife—
text.KJV: But he that is married careth for the things that are of the world, how he may please his wife.
text.NABRE: But a married man is anxious about the things of the world, how he may please his wife,
text.DRB: But he that is with a wife, is careful for the things that pertain to the world, how he may please his wife: and he is divided.

text.NRSV: and his interests are divided. And the unmarried woman and the virgin are anxious about the affairs of the Lord, so that they may be holy in body and spirit; but the married woman is anxious about the affairs of the world, how to please her husband.
text.Rotherham: And he is divided; and, the unmarried woman, or the virgin, is anxious for the things of the Lord, that she may be holy [both] in her body and in her spirit; but, she that hath married, is anxious for the things of the world, how she may please her husband.
text.KJV: There is difference also between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman careth for the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in spirit: but she that is married careth for the things of the world, how she may please her husband.
text.NABRE: and he is divided. An unmarried woman or a virgin is anxious about the things of the Lord, so that she may be holy in both body and spirit. A married woman, on the other hand, is anxious about the things of the world, how she may please her husband.
text.DRB: And the woman unmarried & the virgin, thinketh on the things that pertain to our Lord: that she may be holy both in body and in spirit. But she that is married, thinketh on the things that pertain to the world, how she may please her husband.

text.NRSV: I say this for your own benefit, not to put any restraint upon you, but to promote good order and unhindered devotion to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: This, however, with a view to your own profit, am I saying,—not that, a snare, upon you, I may cast, but with a view to what is comely, and devoted unto the Lord, without distraction.
text.KJV: And this I speak for your own profit; not that I may cast a snare upon you, but for that which is comely, and that ye may attend upon the Lord without distraction.
text.NABRE: I am telling you this for your own benefit, not to impose a restraint upon you, but for the sake of propriety and adherence to the Lord without distraction.
text.DRB: And this I speak to your profit: not to cast a snare upon you, but to that which is honest, & that may give you power without impediment to attend upon our Lord.

text.NRSV: If anyone thinks that he is not behaving properly toward his fiancée, if his passions are strong, and so it has to be, let him marry as he wishes; it is no sin. Let them marry.
text.Rotherham: If however anyone considereth it behaving unseemly towards his virginity, if he should be beyond the bloom of life,—and, thus, it ought to come about, what he chooseth, let him do,—he sinneth not: let them marry:
text.KJV: But if any man think that he behaveth himself uncomely toward his virgin, if she pass the flower of her age, and need so require, let him do what he will, he sinneth not: let them marry.
text.NABRE: If anyone thinks he is behaving improperly toward his virgin, and if a critical moment has come and so it has to be, let him do as he wishes. He is committing no sin; let them get married.
text.DRB: But if any man think that he seemeth dishonoured upon his virgin, for that she is past age, and if it must so be, let him do that he will. He sinneth not if she marry.

text.NRSV: But if someone stands firm in his resolve, being under no necessity but having his own desire under control, and has determined in his own mind to keep her as his fiancée, he will do well.
text.Rotherham: But, he that standeth in his heart, steadfast, having no necessity, but hath authority concerning his own will, and, this, hath determined in his own heart,—to preserve his own virginity, well shall he do.
text.KJV: Nevertheless he that standeth stedfast in his heart, having no necessity, but hath power over his own will, and hath so decreed in his heart that he will keep his virgin, doeth well.
text.NABRE: The one who stands firm in his resolve, however, who is not under compulsion but has power over his own will, and has made up his mind to keep his virgin, will be doing well.
text.DRB: For he that hath determined in his heart being settled, not having necessity, but having power of his own will, and hath judged this in his heart, to keep his virgin, doeth well.

text.NRSV: So then, he who marries his fiancée does well; and he who refrains from marriage will do better.
text.Rotherham: So that, he that giveth in marriage his own virginity, doeth, well; and, he that giveth it not, shall do, better.
text.KJV: So then he that giveth her in marriage doeth well; but he that giveth her not in marriage doeth better.
text.NABRE: So then, the one who marries his virgin does well; the one who does not marry her will do better.
text.DRB: Therefore both he that joineth his virgin in matrimony, doeth well: and he that joineth not, doeth better.

text.NRSV: A wife is bound as long as her husband lives. But if the husband dies, she is free to marry anyone she wishes, only in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: A wife, is bound for as long a time as her husband is living; but, if the husband have fallen asleep, she is, free, to be married unto whom she pleaseth,—only, in the Lord;
text.KJV: The wife is bound by the law as long as her husband liveth; but if her husband be dead, she is at liberty to be married to whom she will; only in the Lord.
text.NABRE: A wife is bound to her husband as long as he lives. But if her husband dies, she is free to be married to whomever she wishes, provided that it be in the Lord.
text.DRB: A woman is bound to the law so long time as her husband liveth: but if her husband sleep, she is at liberty: let her marry to whom she will: only in our Lord.

text.NRSV: But in my judgment she is more blessed if she remains as she is. And I think that I too have the Spirit of God.
text.Rotherham: But, happier, is she, if, so, she remain,—in my judgment; for I think, I also, have the Spirit of God.
text.KJV: But she is happier if she so abide, after my judgment: and I think also that I have the Spirit of God.
text.NABRE: She is more blessed, though, in my opinion, if she remains as she is, and I think that I too have the Spirit of God.
text.DRB: But more blessed shall she be if she so remain, according to my counsel. And I think that I also have the Spirit of God.

text.NRSV: Now concerning food sacrificed to idols: we know that “all of us possess knowledge.” Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.
text.Rotherham: Now, concerning the idol-sacrifices, we are aware—because we, all, have knowledge,—knowledge, puffeth up, but, love, buildeth up;
text.KJV: Now as touching things offered unto idols, we know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffeth up, but charity edifieth.
text.NABRE: B. Offerings to Idols Chapter 8 Knowledge Insufficient. Now in regard to meat sacrificed to idols: we realize that “all of us have knowledge”; knowledge inflates with pride, but love builds up.
text.DRB: And concerning those things that are sacrificed to idols, we know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffeth up; but charity edifieth.

text.NRSV: Anyone who claims to know something does not yet have the necessary knowledge;
text.Rotherham: If anyone thinketh that he knoweth anything, not yet knoweth he, as he must needs come to know,
text.KJV: And if any man think that he knoweth any thing, he knoweth nothing yet as he ought to know.
text.NABRE: If anyone supposes he knows something, he does not yet know as he ought to know.
text.DRB: And if any man think that he knoweth something, he hath not yet known, as he ought to know.

text.NRSV: but anyone who loves God is known by him.
text.Rotherham: But, if anyone loveth God, the same, is known of him—
text.KJV: But if any man love God, the same is known of him.
text.NABRE: But if one loves God, one is known by him.
text.DRB: But if any man love God, the same is known of him.

text.NRSV: Hence, as to the eating of food offered to idols, we know that “no idol in the world really exists,” and that “there is no God but one.”
text.Rotherham: Concerning, then, the eating of idol-sacrifices, we are aware—that an idol is, nothing, in the world, and that, none, is God save one.
text.KJV: As concerning therefore the eating of those things that are offered in sacrifice unto idols, we know that an idol is nothing in the world, and that there is none other God but one.
text.NABRE: So about the eating of meat sacrificed to idols: we know that “there is no idol in the world,” and that “there is no God but one.”
text.DRB: But as for the meats that are immolated to idols, we know that an idol is nothing in the world, and that there is no God, but one.

text.NRSV: Indeed, even though there may be so-called gods in heaven or on earth—as in fact there are many gods and many lords—
text.Rotherham: For, if indeed there are so-called gods, whether in heaven or upon earth,—as indeed there are gods many and lords many,
text.KJV: For though there be that are called gods, whether in heaven or in earth, (as there be gods many, and lords many,)
text.NABRE: Indeed, even though there are so-called gods in heaven and on earth (there are, to be sure, many “gods” and many “lords”),
text.DRB: For although there be that are called gods, either in Heaven, or in earth (for there are many gods, and many lords)

text.NRSV: yet for us there is one God, the Father, from whom are all things and for whom we exist, and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things and through whom we exist.
text.Rotherham: [Yet], to us, there is one God the Father, of whom are all things, and, we, for him; and one Lord Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and, we, through him.
text.KJV: But to us there is but one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we in him; and one Lord Jesus Christ, by whom are all things, and we by him.
text.NABRE: yet for us there is one God, the Father, from whom all things are and for whom we exist, and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom all things are and through whom we exist.
text.DRB: yet to us there is one God, the Father, of whom all things, and we unto him: and one Lord, Jesus Christ, by whom all things, and we by him.

text.NRSV: It is not everyone, however, who has this knowledge. Since some have become so accustomed to idols until now, they still think of the food they eat as food offered to an idol; and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, not in all, is the knowledge; but, some, by their familiarity, until even now, with the idol, as an idol-sacrifice, eat it, and, their conscience, being, weak, is defiled.
text.KJV: Howbeit there is not in every man that knowledge: for some with conscience of the idol unto this hour eat it as a thing offered unto an idol; and their conscience being weak is defiled.
text.NABRE: Practical Rules. But not all have this knowledge. There are some who have been so used to idolatry up until now that, when they eat meat sacrificed to idols, their conscience, which is weak, is defiled.
text.DRB: But there is not knowledge in all. For some until this present with a conscience of the idol, eat as a thing sacrificed to idols: and their conscience being weak, is polluted.

text.NRSV: “Food will not bring us close to God.” We are no worse off if we do not eat, and no better off if we do.
text.Rotherham: Food, indeed, will not commend us unto God,—neither, if we eat not, do we lack, nor, if we eat, do we abound.
text.KJV: But meat commendeth us not to God: for neither, if we eat, are we the better; neither, if we eat not, are we the worse.
text.NABRE: Now food will not bring us closer to God. We are no worse off if we do not eat, nor are we better off if we do.
text.DRB: But meat doth not commend us to God. For neither if we eat, shall we abound: nor if we eat not, shall we lack.

text.NRSV: But take care that this liberty of yours does not somehow become a stumbling block to the weak.
text.Rotherham: But be taking heed, lest, by any means, your right, itself, become, an occasion of stumbling, unto the weak;
text.KJV: But take heed lest by any means this liberty of yours become a stumblingblock to them that are weak.
text.NABRE: But make sure that this liberty of yours in no way becomes a stumbling block to the weak.
text.DRB: But take heed lest perhaps this your liberty become an offence to the weak.

text.NRSV: For if others see you, who possess knowledge, eating in the temple of an idol, might they not, since their conscience is weak, be encouraged to the point of eating food sacrificed to idols?
text.Rotherham: For, if anyone should see [thee] who hast knowledge, in an idol-temple, reclining, will not, his conscience, being, weak, be built up for the eating of the idol-sacrifices?
text.KJV: For if any man see thee which hast knowledge sit at meat in the idol’s temple, shall not the conscience of him which is weak be emboldened to eat those things which are offered to idols;
text.NABRE: If someone sees you, with your knowledge, reclining at table in the temple of an idol, may not his conscience too, weak as it is, be “built up” to eat the meat sacrificed to idols?
text.DRB: For if a man see him that hath knowledge, sit at table in the idol's temple; shall not his conscience, being weak, be edified, to eat things sacrificed to idols?

text.NRSV: So by your knowledge those weak believers for whom Christ died are destroyed.
text.Rotherham: In fact, he that is weak, is being destroyed, by thy knowledge—the brother for whose sake Christ died.
text.KJV: And through thy knowledge shall the weak brother perish, for whom Christ died?
text.NABRE: Thus through your knowledge, the weak person is brought to destruction, the brother for whom Christ died.
text.DRB: And through thy knowledge shall the weak brother perish, for whom Christ hath died?

text.NRSV: But when you thus sin against members of your family, and wound their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.
text.Rotherham: But, in thus sinning against the brethren am wounding their conscience, seeing it is weak, against Christ, are ye sinning.
text.KJV: But when ye sin so against the brethren, and wound their weak conscience, ye sin against Christ.
text.NABRE: When you sin in this way against your brothers and wound their consciences, weak as they are, you are sinning against Christ.
text.DRB: But sinning thus against the brethren, and striking their weak conscience; you sin against Christ.

text.NRSV: Therefore, if food causes my brother or sister to fall, I will never eat meat, so that I may not cause one of them to fall.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, if food is an occasion of stumbling unto my brother, in nowise will I eat flesh unto the age that abideth,—that, I may not occasion, my brother, to stumble.
text.KJV: Wherefore, if meat make my brother to offend, I will eat no flesh while the world standeth, lest I make my brother to offend.
text.NABRE: Therefore, if food causes my brother to sin, I will never eat meat again, so that I may not cause my brother to sin.
text.DRB: Wherefore if meat scandalize my brother, I will never eat flesh, lest I scandalize my brother.

text.NRSV: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
text.Rotherham: Am not free? Am I not an apostle? Jesus our Lord, have I not seen? Are not, ye, my work, in the Lord?
text.KJV: Am I not an apostle? am I not free? have I not seen Jesus Christ our Lord? are not ye my work in the Lord?
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 Paul’s Rights as an Apostle. Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
text.DRB: AM I not free? Am I not an Apostle? Have I not seen Christ Jesus our Lord? Are not you my work in our Lord?

text.NRSV: If I am not an apostle to others, at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: If, unto others, I am not an apostle, certainly at least, unto you, I am; for, the seal of my apostleship, ye, are in the Lord.
text.KJV: If I be not an apostle unto others, yet doubtless I am to you: for the seal of mine apostleship are ye in the Lord.
text.NABRE: Although I may not be an apostle for others, certainly I am for you, for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
text.DRB: And if to others I be not an Apostle, but yet to you I am. For you are the seal of my Apostleship in our Lord.

text.NRSV: This is my defense to those who would examine me.
text.Rotherham: My defence, unto them who are examining me, is this:
text.KJV: Mine answer to them that do examine me is this,
text.NABRE: My defense against those who would pass judgment on me is this.
text.DRB: my defence to them that examine me is this.

text.NRSV: Do we not have the right to our food and drink?
text.Rotherham: Have we not a right to eat and drink?
text.KJV: Have we not power to eat and to drink?
text.NABRE: Do we not have the right to eat and drink?
text.DRB: Have not we power to eat and drink?

text.NRSV: Do we not have the right to be accompanied by a believing wife, as do the other apostles and the brothers of the Lord and Cephas?
text.Rotherham: Have we not a right to take round, a sister wife,—as even the rest of the apostles, and the brethren of the Lord, and Cephas?
text.KJV: Have we not power to lead about a sister, a wife, as well as other apostles, and as the brethren of the Lord, and Cephas?
text.NABRE: Do we not have the right to take along a Christian wife, as do the rest of the apostles, and the brothers of the Lord, and Cephas?
text.DRB: Have we not power to lead about a woman a sister, as also the rest of the Apostles, and our Lord's brethren, and Cephas?

text.NRSV: Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working for a living?
text.Rotherham: Or have, only I and Barnabas, not a right to forbear working?
text.KJV: Or I only and Barnabas, have not we power to forbear working?
text.NABRE: Or is it only myself and Barnabas who do not have the right not to work?
text.DRB: Or I only and Barnabas have not we power to do this?

text.NRSV: Who at any time pays the cost of his own military service? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat any of its fruit? Or who tends a flock and does not get any of its milk?
text.Rotherham: Who serveth as a soldier, at his own charges, at any time? Who planteth a vineyard,—and, the fruit thereof, doth not eat? [Or] who shepherdeth a flock,—and, of the milk of the flock, doth not eat?
text.KJV: Who goeth a warfare any time at his own charges? who planteth a vineyard, and eateth not of the fruit thereof? or who feedeth a flock, and eateth not of the milk of the flock?
text.NABRE: Who ever serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard without eating its produce? Or who shepherds a flock without using some of the milk from the flock?
text.DRB: Who ever playeth the soldier at his own charges? who planteth a vine, and eateth not of the fruit thereof? Who feedeth a flock, and eateth not of the milk of the flock?

text.NRSV: Do I say this on human authority? Does not the law also say the same?
text.Rotherham: Is it, after the manner of men, that these things I am saying? Or doth not, even the law, the same things, say?
text.KJV: Say I these things as a man? or saith not the law the same also?
text.NABRE: Am I saying this on human authority, or does not the law also speak of these things?
text.DRB: Speak I these things according to man? Or doth not the Law also say these things?

text.NRSV: For it is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain.” Is it for oxen that God is concerned?
text.Rotherham: For, in the law of Moses, it is written—Thou shalt not muzzle an ox when it is treading out the corn:—Is it, for the oxen, God is caring?
text.KJV: For it is written in the law of Moses, Thou shalt not muzzle the mouth of the ox that treadeth out the corn. Doth God take care for oxen?
text.NABRE: It is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain.” Is God concerned about oxen,
text.DRB: For it is written in the Law of Moyses: Thou shalt not muzzle the mouth of the ox that treadeth out the corn. Why, hath God care of oxen?

text.NRSV: Or does he not speak entirely for our sake? It was indeed written for our sake, for the plowman should plow in hope and the thresher thresh in hope of a share in the crop.
text.Rotherham: Or, for our sakes altogether, is he saying it? For our sakes, it was written; because, he that ploweth, ought, to plow, in hope,—and, he that thresheth, [to thresh] in hope of partaking.
text.KJV: Or saith he it altogether for our sakes? For our sakes, no doubt, this is written: that he that ploweth should plow in hope; and that he that thresheth in hope should be partaker of his hope.
text.NABRE: or is he not really speaking for our sake? It was written for our sake, because the plowman should plow in hope, and the thresher in hope of receiving a share.
text.DRB: Or for us certes doth he say it? For they are written for us. Because he that eareth, ought to ear in hope: and he that treadeth, in hope to receive fruit.

text.NRSV: If we have sown spiritual good among you, is it too much if we reap your material benefits?
text.Rotherham: If, we, unto you, the things of the Spirit have sown, is it a great matter, if, we, of you, the things of the flesh shall reap?
text.KJV: If we have sown unto you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we shall reap your carnal things?
text.NABRE: If we have sown spiritual seed for you, is it a great thing that we reap a material harvest from you?
text.DRB: If we have sown unto you spiritual things, is it a great matter if we reap your carnal things?

text.NRSV: If others share this rightful claim on you, do not we still more? Nevertheless, we have not made use of this right, but we endure anything rather than put an obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
text.Rotherham: If, others, of this right, over you, are partaking, [should] not rather, we? nevertheless, we used not this right; but, all [such] things, do we conceal,—lest we should cause any hindrance unto the glad message of the Christ.
text.KJV: If others be partakers of this power over you, are not we rather? Nevertheless we have not used this power; but suffer all things, lest we should hinder the gospel of Christ.
text.NABRE: If others share this rightful claim on you, do not we still more? Reason for Not Using His Rights. Yet we have not used this right. On the contrary, we endure everything so as not to place an obstacle to the gospel of Christ.
text.DRB: If other be partakers of your power; why not we rather? Howbeit we have not used, this power: but we bear all things, lest we should give any offence to the Gospel of Christ.

text.NRSV: Do you not know that those who are employed in the temple service get their food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share in what is sacrificed on the altar?
text.Rotherham: Know ye not that, they who labour in the holy rites, do eat the provisions out of the holy place? They who at the altar wait, do, with the altar, share?
text.KJV: Do ye not know that they which minister about holy things live of the things of the temple? and they which wait at the altar are partakers with the altar?
text.NABRE: Do you not know that those who perform the temple services eat [what] belongs to the temple, and those who minister at the altar share in the sacrificial offerings?
text.DRB: Know you not that they which work in the holy place, eat the things that are of the holy place: and they that serve the altar, participate with the altar?

text.NRSV: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who proclaim the gospel should get their living by the gospel.
text.Rotherham: Thus, also, hath the Lord, appointed—that, they who the glad message tell, should, of the glad-message, live.
text.KJV: Even so hath the Lord ordained that they which preach the gospel should live of the gospel.
text.NABRE: In the same way, the Lord ordered that those who preach the gospel should live by the gospel.
text.DRB: So also our Lord ordained for them that preach the Gospel, to live of the Gospel.

text.NRSV: But I have made no use of any of these rights, nor am I writing this so that they may be applied in my case. Indeed, I would rather die than that—no one will deprive me of my ground for boasting!
text.Rotherham: I, however, have not used any of these things; and have not written these things, in order that, so it should be done, in my case; for it were, good for me, rather to die than——my boast, shall, no man, make void!
text.KJV: But I have used none of these things: neither have I written these things, that it should be so done unto me: for it were better for me to die, than that any man should make my glorying void.
text.NABRE: I have not used any of these rights, however, nor do I write this that it be done so in my case. I would rather die. Certainly no one is going to nullify my boast.
text.DRB: But I have used none of these. Neither have I written these things, that they should be so done in me; for it is good for me to die rather, than that any man should make my glory void.

text.NRSV: If I proclaim the gospel, this gives me no ground for boasting, for an obligation is laid on me, and woe to me if I do not proclaim the gospel!
text.Rotherham: For, if I be telling the glad-message, it is, with me, no matter of boasting; for, necessity, upon me, lieth,—for it is, Woe to me, if I should not be telling the glad-message;
text.KJV: For though I preach the gospel, I have nothing to glory of: for necessity is laid upon me; yea, woe is unto me, if I preach not the gospel!
text.NABRE: If I preach the gospel, this is no reason for me to boast, for an obligation has been imposed on me, and woe to me if I do not preach it!
text.DRB: For & if I evangelize, it is no glory to me: for necessity lieth upon me: for woe is to me if I evangelize not.

text.NRSV: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward; but if not of my own will, I am entrusted with a commission.
text.Rotherham: For, if, by choice, this thing I am doing, I have, a reward; but, if not by choice, with a stewardship, have I been entrusted!
text.KJV: For if I do this thing willingly, I have a reward: but if against my will, a dispensation of the gospel is committed unto me.
text.NABRE: If I do so willingly, I have a recompense, but if unwillingly, then I have been entrusted with a stewardship.
text.DRB: For if I do this willingly, I have reward: but if against my will, a charge is committed to me.

text.NRSV: What then is my reward? Just this: that in my proclamation I may make the gospel free of charge, so as not to make full use of my rights in the gospel.
text.Rotherham: What, then, is my reward? that, in telling the glad-message, free of cost, I should put the glad-message,—to the end I should not use to the full my right in the glad-message.
text.KJV: What is my reward then? Verily that, when I preach the gospel, I may make the gospel of Christ without charge, that I abuse not my power in the gospel.
text.NABRE: What then is my recompense? That, when I preach, I offer the gospel free of charge so as not to make full use of my right in the gospel.
text.DRB: What is my reward then? That preaching the Gospel, I yield the Gospel without cost, that I abuse not my power in the Gospel.

text.NRSV: For though I am free with respect to all, I have made myself a slave to all, so that I might win more of them.
text.Rotherham: For, though free from all, unto all, myself, I enslaved,—that, the larger number, I might win:
text.KJV: For though I be free from all men, yet have I made myself servant unto all, that I might gain the more.
text.NABRE: All Things to All. Although I am free in regard to all, I have made myself a slave to all so as to win over as many as possible.
text.DRB: For whereas I was free of all, I made myself the servant of all: that I might gain the more.

text.NRSV: To the Jews I became as a Jew, in order to win Jews. To those under the law I became as one under the law (though I myself am not under the law) so that I might win those under the law.
text.Rotherham: Therefore became I, to the Jews, as, a Jew,—that, Jews, I might win; to them who were under law, as, under law, not being, myself, under law,—that, them who were under law, I might win;
text.KJV: And unto the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might gain the Jews; to them that are under the law, as under the law, that I might gain them that are under the law;
text.NABRE: To the Jews I became like a Jew to win over Jews; to those under the law I became like one under the law—though I myself am not under the law—to win over those under the law.
text.DRB: And I became to the Jews as a Jew, that I might gain the Jews.

text.NRSV: To those outside the law I became as one outside the law (though I am not free from God’s law but am under Christ’s law) so that I might win those outside the law.
text.Rotherham: To them who were without law, as, without law,—not being without law to God, but lawfully subject to Christ,—that I might win them who were without law.
text.KJV: To them that are without law, as without law, (being not without law to God, but under the law to Christ,) that I might gain them that are without law.
text.NABRE: To those outside the law I became like one outside the law—though I am not outside God’s law but within the law of Christ—to win over those outside the law.
text.DRB: To them that are under the Law, as though I were under the Law (whereas myself was not under the Law) that I might gain them that were under the Law. To them that were without the Law, as though I were without the Law (whereas I was not without the law of God, but was in the law of Christ) that I might gain them that were without the Law.

text.NRSV: To the weak I became weak, so that I might win the weak. I have become all things to all people, that I might by all means save some.
text.Rotherham: I became, unto the weak, weak,—that, the weak, I might win;—to all men, have I become all things, that, by all means, some, I might save.
text.KJV: To the weak became I as weak, that I might gain the weak: I am made all things to all men, that I might by all means save some.
text.NABRE: To the weak I became weak, to win over the weak. I have become all things to all, to save at least some.
text.DRB: To the weak I became weak, that I might gain the weak. To all men I became all things, that I might save all.

text.NRSV: I do it all for the sake of the gospel, so that I may share in its blessings.
text.Rotherham: But, all things, am I doing, for the sake of the glad-message, that a joint-partaker thereof, I may become.
text.KJV: And this I do for the gospel’s sake, that I might be partaker thereof with you.
text.NABRE: All this I do for the sake of the gospel, so that I too may have a share in it.
text.DRB: And I do all things for the Gospel, that I may be made partaker thereof.

text.NRSV: Do you not know that in a race the runners all compete, but only one receives the prize? Run in such a way that you may win it.
text.Rotherham: Know ye not that, they who, in a racecourse, run, all, indeed, run,—but, one, receiveth the prize? So, be running, that ye may lay hold.
text.KJV: Know ye not that they which run in a race run all, but one receiveth the prize? So run, that ye may obtain.
text.NABRE: Do you not know that the runners in the stadium all run in the race, but only one wins the prize? Run so as to win.
text.DRB: Know you not that they that run in the race, all run indeed, but one receiveth the prize? So run that you may obtain.

text.NRSV: Athletes exercise self-control in all things; they do it to receive a perishable wreath, but we an imperishable one.
text.Rotherham: But, every man who striveth in the games, in all things, useth self-control;—they, indeed, then, that a corruptible crown, they may receive; but, we, an incorruptible!
text.KJV: And every man that striveth for the mastery is temperate in all things. Now they do it to obtain a corruptible crown; but we an incorruptible.
text.NABRE: Every athlete exercises discipline in every way. They do it to win a perishable crown, but we an imperishable one.
text.DRB: And every one the striveth for the mastery, refraineth himself from all things: and they certes, that they may receive a corruptible crown: but we an incorruptible.

text.NRSV: So I do not run aimlessly, nor do I box as though beating the air;
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, so, am running, as, not uncertainly, so, am boxing, as not thrashing air;
text.KJV: I therefore so run, not as uncertainly; so fight I, not as one that beateth the air:
text.NABRE: Thus I do not run aimlessly; I do not fight as if I were shadowboxing.
text.DRB: Itherefore so run, not as it were at an uncertain thing: so I fight, not as it were beating the air:

text.NRSV: but I punish my body and enslave it, so that after proclaiming to others I myself should not be disqualified.
text.Rotherham: But am beating my body under, and leading it captive, lest, by any means,—unto others, having proclaimed, [the contest], I myself, should be rejected.
text.KJV: But I keep under my body, and bring it into subjection: lest that by any means, when I have preached to others, I myself should be a castaway.
text.NABRE: No, I drive my body and train it, for fear that, after having preached to others, I myself should be disqualified.
text.DRB: But I chastise my body, and bring it into servitude, lest perhaps when I have preached to others, myself become reprobate.

text.NRSV: I do not want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, that our ancestors were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea,
text.Rotherham: For I wish not ye should be ignorant, brethren, that, all our fathers, were, under the cloud, and, all, passed through the sea,—
text.KJV: Moreover, brethren, I would not that ye should be ignorant, how that all our fathers were under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 Warning Against Overconfidence. I do not want you to be unaware, brothers, that our ancestors were all under the cloud and all passed through the sea,
text.DRB: For I will not have you ignorant, brethren, that our fathers were all under the cloud, & all passed through the sea,

text.NRSV: and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea,
text.Rotherham: And, all, immersed themselves into Moses, in the cloud, and in the sea;
text.KJV: And were all baptized unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
text.NABRE: and all of them were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
text.DRB: and all in Moyses were baptized in the cloud and in the sea:

text.NRSV: and all ate the same spiritual food,
text.Rotherham: And, all, did eat [the same] spiritual food,
text.KJV: And did all eat the same spiritual meat;
text.NABRE: All ate the same spiritual food,
text.DRB: and all did eat the same spiritual food,

text.NRSV: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank from the spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
text.Rotherham: And, all, drank, the same spiritual drink,—for they continued to drink of the spiritual rock that followed them, and, the rock, was the Christ:—
text.KJV: And did all drink the same spiritual drink: for they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them: and that Rock was Christ.
text.NABRE: and all drank the same spiritual drink, for they drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was the Christ.
text.DRB: and all drunk the same spiritual drink (and they drunk of the spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.)

text.NRSV: Nevertheless, God was not pleased with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, with the most of them, God, was not well-pleased, for they were strewed along in the desert.
text.KJV: But with many of them God was not well pleased: for they were overthrown in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Yet God was not pleased with most of them, for they were struck down in the desert.
text.DRB: But in the more part of them God was not well pleased. For they were overthrown in the desert.

text.NRSV: Now these things occurred as examples for us, so that we might not desire evil as they did.
text.Rotherham: But, in these things, they became, ensamples for us, to the end we should not be covetous of evil things, even as, they, also coveted;
text.KJV: Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted.
text.NABRE: These things happened as examples for us, so that we might not desire evil things, as they did.
text.DRB: And these things were done in a figure of us, that we be not coveting evil things, as they also coveted.

text.NRSV: Do not become idolaters as some of them did; as it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and they rose up to play.”
text.Rotherham: Neither become ye, idolaters, as some of them,—as it is written—The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to be making sport;
text.KJV: Neither be ye idolaters, as were some of them; as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.
text.NABRE: And do not become idolaters, as some of them did, as it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to revel.”
text.DRB: Neither become ye idolaters, as certain of them: as is written: The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.

text.NRSV: We must not indulge in sexual immorality as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell in a single day.
text.Rotherham: Neither let us commit fornication, as, some of them, committed fornication, and there fell, in one day, three and twenty thousand;
text.KJV: Neither let us commit fornication, as some of them committed, and fell in one day three and twenty thousand.
text.NABRE: Let us not indulge in immorality as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell within a single day.
text.DRB: Neither let us fornicate, as certain of them did fornicate, and there fell in one day three and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: We must not put Christ to the test, as some of them did, and were destroyed by serpents.
text.Rotherham: Neither let us be putting the Lord to the test, as, some of them, put him to the test, and, by the serpents, were perishing;
text.KJV: Neither let us tempt Christ, as some of them also tempted, and were destroyed of serpents.
text.NABRE: Let us not test Christ as some of them did, and suffered death by serpents.
text.DRB: Neither let us tempt Christ, as certain of them tempted, and perished by the serpents.

text.NRSV: And do not complain as some of them did, and were destroyed by the destroyer.
text.Rotherham: Neither be ye murmuring, even, as some of them, murmured, and perished by the destroyer.
text.KJV: Neither murmur ye, as some of them also murmured, and were destroyed of the destroyer.
text.NABRE: Do not grumble as some of them did, and suffered death by the destroyer.
text.DRB: Neither do you murmur, as certain of them murmured, and perished by the destroyer.

text.NRSV: These things happened to them to serve as an example, and they were written down to instruct us, on whom the ends of the ages have come.
text.Rotherham: But, these things, by way of type, were happening unto them, and were written with a view to our admonition, unto whom, the ends of the ages, have reached along.
text.KJV: Now all these things happened unto them for ensamples: and they are written for our admonition, upon whom the ends of the world are come.
text.NABRE: These things happened to them as an example, and they have been written down as a warning to us, upon whom the end of the ages has come.
text.DRB: And all these things chanced to them in figure: but they are written to our correption, upon whom the ends of the world are come.

text.NRSV: So if you think you are standing, watch out that you do not fall.
text.Rotherham: So that, he that thinketh he is standing, let him take heed lest he fall.
text.KJV: Wherefore let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall.
text.NABRE: Therefore, whoever thinks he is standing secure should take care not to fall.
text.DRB: Therefore he that thinketh himself to stand, let him take heed lest he fall.

text.NRSV: No testing has overtaken you that is not common to everyone. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tested beyond your strength, but with the testing he will also provide the way out so that you may be able to endure it.
text.Rotherham: Trial, hath not taken you, save such as man can bear; faithful, moreover is God, who will not suffer you to be tried above what ye are able, but will make, with the trial, also the way of escape, that ye may be able to hold out.
text.KJV: There hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able; but will with the temptation also make a way to escape, that ye may be able to bear it.
text.NABRE: No trial has come to you but what is human. God is faithful and will not let you be tried beyond your strength; but with the trial he will also provide a way out, so that you may be able to bear it. Warning Against Idolatry.
text.DRB: Let not tentation apprehend you, but human. And God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above that which you are able: but will make also with tentation issue, that you may be able to sustain.

text.NRSV: Therefore, my dear friends, flee from the worship of idols.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry,—
text.KJV: Wherefore, my dearly beloved, flee from idolatry.
text.NABRE: Therefore, my beloved, avoid idolatry.
text.DRB: For the which cause, my dearest, fly from the serving of idols.

text.NRSV: I speak as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
text.Rotherham: As, to prudent men, I speak,—judge, ye, what I say:—
text.KJV: I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
text.NABRE: I am speaking as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I am saying.
text.DRB: Ispeak as to wise men: yourselves judge what I say.

text.NRSV: The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a sharing in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a sharing in the body of Christ?
text.Rotherham: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not, a sharing together of the blood of the Christ? The loaf which we break, is it not, a sharing together of the body of the Christ?
text.KJV: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
text.NABRE: The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a participation in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a participation in the body of Christ?
text.DRB: The chalice of benediction which we do bless, is it not the communication of the blood of Christ? and the bread which we break, is it not the participation of the body of our Lord?

text.NRSV: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread.
text.Rotherham: Because, one loaf, one body, we, the many, are, for we, all, of the one loaf, partake.
text.KJV: For we being many are one bread, and one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.
text.NABRE: Because the loaf of bread is one, we, though many, are one body, for we all partake of the one loaf.
text.DRB: For being many, we are one bread, one body, all that participate of one bread.

text.NRSV: Consider the people of Israel; are not those who eat the sacrifices partners in the altar?
text.Rotherham: Be looking at Israel after the flesh:—are not, they who eat the sacrifices, joint partakers with the altar?
text.KJV: Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
text.NABRE: Look at Israel according to the flesh; are not those who eat the sacrifices participants in the altar?
text.DRB: Behold Israel according to the flesh: they that eat the hosts, are they not partakers of the altar?

text.NRSV: What do I imply then? That food sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
text.Rotherham: What, then, am I saying?—that, an idol-sacrifice, is anything? or that, an idol, is anything?
text.KJV: What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing?
text.NABRE: So what am I saying? That meat sacrificed to idols is anything? Or that an idol is anything?
text.DRB: What then? do I say that that which is immolated to idols, is any thing? or that the idol is any thing?

text.NRSV: No, I imply that what pagans sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be partners with demons.
text.Rotherham: On the contrary—that, the things which the nations sacrifice, unto demons, and not unto God, they sacrifice; and I wish not that ye should become, sharers together with the demons!
text.KJV: But I say, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God: and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.
text.NABRE: No, I mean that what they sacrifice, [they sacrifice] to demons, not to God, and I do not want you to become participants with demons.
text.DRB: But the things that the heathen do immolate, to devils they do immolate, and not to God. And I will not have you become fellows of devils.

text.NRSV: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
text.Rotherham: Ye cannot be drinking—a cup of the Lord, and a cup of demons; ye cannot be partaking of, a table of the Lord, and a table of demons.
text.KJV: Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord’s table, and of the table of devils.
text.NABRE: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and also the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and of the table of demons.
text.DRB: You cannot drink the chalice of our Lord, and the chalice of devils: you cannot be partakers of the table of our Lord, and of the table of devils.

text.NRSV: Or are we provoking the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
text.Rotherham: Or are we to provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we mightier than he?
text.KJV: Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?
text.NABRE: Or are we provoking the Lord to jealous anger? Are we stronger than he? Seek the Good of Others.
text.DRB: Or do we emulate our Lord? Why, are we stronger than he? All things are lawful for me, but all things are not expedient.

text.NRSV: “All things are lawful,” but not all things are beneficial. “All things are lawful,” but not all things build up.
text.Rotherham: All things are allowable—but, not all things, are profitable,—all things are allowable—but, not all things, upbuild.
text.KJV: All things are lawful for me, but all things are not expedient: all things are lawful for me, but all things edify not.
text.NABRE: “Everything is lawful,” but not everything is beneficial. “Everything is lawful,” but not everything builds up.
text.DRB: All things are lawful for me, but all things do not edify.

text.NRSV: Do not seek your own advantage, but that of others.
text.Rotherham: Let, no one, be seeking, what concerneth himself, but that which concerneth some one else.
text.KJV: Let no man seek his own, but every man another’s wealth.
text.NABRE: No one should seek his own advantage, but that of his neighbor.
text.DRB: Let no man seek his own, but another man's.

text.NRSV: Eat whatever is sold in the meat market without raising any question on the ground of conscience,
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever, in the market, is sold, eat,—asking no question, for conscience sake;
text.KJV: Whatsoever is sold in the shambles, that eat, asking no question for conscience sake:
text.NABRE: Eat anything sold in the market, without raising questions on grounds of conscience,
text.DRB: All that is sold in the shambles, eat: asking no question for conscience.

text.NRSV: for “the earth and its fullness are the Lord’s.”
text.Rotherham: For, unto the Lord, belongeth the earth, and the fullness thereof.
text.KJV: For the earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof.
text.NABRE: for “the earth and its fullness are the Lord’s.”
text.DRB: The earth is our Lord's, and the fullness thereof.

text.NRSV: If an unbeliever invites you to a meal and you are disposed to go, eat whatever is set before you without raising any question on the ground of conscience.
text.Rotherham: If one of them who believe not invite you, and ye are disposed to go, whatsoever is set before you, eat,—asking no question, for conscience sake.
text.KJV: If any of them that believe not bid you to a feast, and ye be disposed to go; whatsoever is set before you, eat, asking no question for conscience sake.
text.NABRE: If an unbeliever invites you and you want to go, eat whatever is placed before you, without raising questions on grounds of conscience.
text.DRB: If any invite you of the infidels, and you will go; eat of all that is set before you, asking no question for conscience.

text.NRSV: But if someone says to you, “This has been offered in sacrifice,” then do not eat it, out of consideration for the one who informed you, and for the sake of conscience—
text.Rotherham: But, if anyone should say unto you,—This, is, a holy sacrifice, do not eat, for that man’s sake who disclosed it, and for conscience sake:—
text.KJV: But if any man say unto you, This is offered in sacrifice unto idols, eat not for his sake that shewed it, and for conscience sake: for the earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof:
text.NABRE: But if someone says to you, “This was offered in sacrifice,” do not eat it on account of the one who called attention to it and on account of conscience;
text.DRB: But if any man say, This is immolated to idols; do not eat for his sake that shewed it, and for conscience:

text.NRSV: I mean the other’s conscience, not your own. For why should my liberty be subject to the judgment of someone else’s conscience?
text.Rotherham: But, conscience, I mean, not thine own, but the other’s,—for why is my freedom to be judged by another’s conscience?
text.KJV: Conscience, I say, not thine own, but of the other: for why is my liberty judged of another man’s conscience?
text.NABRE: I mean not your own conscience, but the other’s. For why should my freedom be determined by someone else’s conscience?
text.DRB: conscience I say not thine but the other's. For why is my liberty judged of another man's conscience?

text.NRSV: If I partake with thankfulness, why should I be reviled because of something for which I give thanks?
text.Rotherham: If, I, with gratitude, partake, why am I to be defamed, as to that for which, I, give thanks?
text.KJV: For if I by grace be a partaker, why am I evil spoken of for that for which I give thanks?
text.NABRE: If I partake thankfully, why am I reviled for that over which I give thanks?
text.DRB: If I participate with thanks; why am I blasphemed for that which I give thanks for?

text.NRSV: So, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do everything for the glory of God.
text.Rotherham: Whether, therefore, ye eat or drink, or whatsoever ye do, do, all things to the glory of God.
text.KJV: Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatsoever ye do, do all to the glory of God.
text.NABRE: So whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do everything for the glory of God.
text.DRB: Therefore whether you eat, or drink, or do any other thing; do all things unto the glory of God.

text.NRSV: Give no offense to Jews or to Greeks or to the church of God,
text.Rotherham: Give no occasion of stumbling—whether to Jews, or to Greeks, or to the assembly of God:
text.KJV: Give none offence, neither to the Jews, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the church of God:
text.NABRE: Avoid giving offense, whether to Jews or Greeks or the church of God,
text.DRB: Be without offence to the Jews & to the Gentiles, & to the Church of God:

text.NRSV: just as I try to please everyone in everything I do, not seeking my own advantage, but that of many, so that they may be saved.
text.Rotherham: Even as, I also, in all things do please all, not seeking my own profit, but that of the many, that they may be saved.
text.KJV: Even as I please all men in all things, not seeking mine own profit, but the profit of many, that they may be saved.
text.NABRE: just as I try to please everyone in every way, not seeking my own benefit but that of the many, that they may be saved.
text.DRB: as I also in all things do please all men, not seeking that which is profitable to myself, but which is to many; that they may be saved.

text.NRSV: Be imitators of me, as I am of Christ.
text.Rotherham: Become imitators of me,—even as, I also, [am] of Christ.
text.KJV: Be ye followers of me, even as I also am of Christ.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 Be imitators of me, as I am of Christ.
text.DRB: BE ye followers of me, as I also of Christ.

text.NRSV: I commend you because you remember me in everything and maintain the traditions just as I handed them on to you.
text.Rotherham: Now I praise you, that, in all things, ye have me in remembrance, and, even as I delivered unto you the instructions, ye hold them fast.
text.KJV: Now I praise you, brethren, that ye remember me in all things, and keep the ordinances, as I delivered them to you.
text.NABRE: IV. Problems in Liturgical Assemblies I praise you because you remember me in everything and hold fast to the traditions, just as I handed them on to you.
text.DRB: And I praise you brethren, that in all things you be mindful of me: and as I have delivered unto you, you keep my precepts.

text.NRSV: But I want you to understand that Christ is the head of every man, and the husband is the head of his wife, and God is the head of Christ.
text.Rotherham: But I wish you to know—that, the head of every man, is, the Christ, and, the head of a woman, is, the man; and, the head of the Christ, is, God.
text.KJV: But I would have you know, that the head of every man is Christ; and the head of the woman is the man; and the head of Christ is God.
text.NABRE: A. Women’s Headdresses Man and Woman. But I want you to know that Christ is the head of every man, and a husband the head of his wife, and God the head of Christ.
text.DRB: And I will have you know, that the head of every man, is Christ: and the head of the woman, is the man: and the head of Christ, is God.

text.NRSV: Any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head disgraces his head,
text.Rotherham: Every man, praying, or prophesying, having anything upon his head, putteth to shame his head;
text.KJV: Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonoureth his head.
text.NABRE: Any man who prays or prophesies with his head covered brings shame upon his head.
text.DRB: Every man praying or prophesying with his head covered, dishonoureth his head.

text.NRSV: but any woman who prays or prophesies with her head unveiled disgraces her head—it is one and the same thing as having her head shaved.
text.Rotherham: But, every woman, praying, or prophesying, with her head, unveiled, putteth to shame her head,—for it is, one and the same, with her having been shaven.
text.KJV: But every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with her head uncovered dishonoureth her head: for that is even all one as if she were shaven.
text.NABRE: But any woman who prays or prophesies with her head unveiled brings shame upon her head, for it is one and the same thing as if she had had her head shaved.
text.DRB: But every woman praying or prophesying with her head not covered, dishonoureth her head: for it is all one as if she were made bald.

text.NRSV: For if a woman will not veil herself, then she should cut off her hair; but if it is disgraceful for a woman to have her hair cut off or she should be shaved, she should wear a veil.
text.Rotherham: For, if a woman doth not veil herself, let her also be shorn; but, if it were a shame in a woman to be shorn or shaven, let her be veiled.
text.KJV: For if the woman be not covered, let her also be shorn: but if it be a shame for a woman to be shorn or shaven, let her be covered.
text.NABRE: For if a woman does not have her head veiled, she may as well have her hair cut off. But if it is shameful for a woman to have her hair cut off or her head shaved, then she should wear a veil.
text.DRB: For if a woman be not covered, let her be polled. But if it be a foul thing for a woman to be polled or made bald, let her cover her head.

text.NRSV: For a man ought not to have his head veiled, since he is the image and reflection of God; but woman is the reflection of man.
text.Rotherham: For, a man, indeed, ought not to be veiling his head, being, the image and glory of God,—but, the woman, is the glory of man;
text.KJV: For a man indeed ought not to cover his head, forasmuch as he is the image and glory of God: but the woman is the glory of the man.
text.NABRE: A man, on the other hand, should not cover his head, because he is the image and glory of God, but woman is the glory of man.
text.DRB: The man truly ought not to cover his head, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is the glory of the man.

text.NRSV: Indeed, man was not made from woman, but woman from man.
text.Rotherham: For, man, is not, of woman, but, woman, of man;
text.KJV: For the man is not of the woman; but the woman of the man.
