text.NABRE: For man did not come from woman, but woman from man;
text.DRB: For the man is not of the woman, but the woman of the man.

text.NRSV: Neither was man created for the sake of woman, but woman for the sake of man.
text.Rotherham: For also man was not created, for the sake of the woman, but woman, for the sake of the man.
text.KJV: Neither was the man created for the woman; but the woman for the man.
text.NABRE: nor was man created for woman, but woman for man;
text.DRB: For the man was not created for the woman, but the woman for the man.

text.NRSV: For this reason a woman ought to have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, ought the woman to have, permission, upon her head, because of the messengers.
text.KJV: For this cause ought the woman to have power on her head because of the angels.
text.NABRE: for this reason a woman should have a sign of authority on her head, because of the angels.
text.DRB: (Therefore ought the woman to have power upon her head for the Angels.)

text.NRSV: Nevertheless, in the Lord woman is not independent of man or man independent of woman.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, neither is woman apart from man, nor man apart from woman—in the Lord;
text.KJV: Nevertheless neither is the man without the woman, neither the woman without the man, in the Lord.
text.NABRE: Woman is not independent of man or man of woman in the Lord.
text.DRB: But yet neither the man without the woman; nor the woman without the man, in our Lord.

text.NRSV: For just as woman came from man, so man comes through woman; but all things come from God.
text.Rotherham: For, just as the woman is of the man, so, also the man, is through means of the woman;—but, all things, are of God.
text.KJV: For as the woman is of the man, even so is the man also by the woman; but all things of God.
text.NABRE: For just as woman came from man, so man is born of woman; but all things are from God.
text.DRB: For as the woman is of the man, so also the man by the woman: but all things of God.

text.NRSV: Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head unveiled?
text.Rotherham: Among your own selves, judge ye,—Is it becoming for a woman,—unveiled, to be praying unto God?
text.KJV: Judge in yourselves: is it comely that a woman pray unto God uncovered?
text.NABRE: Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head unveiled?
text.DRB: Yourselves judge: doth it become a woman not covered to pray unto God?

text.NRSV: Does not nature itself teach you that if a man wears his hair long, it is degrading to him,
text.Rotherham: Doth not, even nature herself, teach you—that, if, a man, have long hair, it is a dishonour to him;
text.KJV: Doth not even nature itself teach you, that, if a man have long hair, it is a shame unto him?
text.NABRE: Does not nature itself teach you that if a man wears his hair long it is a disgrace to him,
text.DRB: Neither doth nature itself teach you, that a man indeed if he nourish his hair, it is an ignominy for him:

text.NRSV: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? For her hair is given to her for a covering.
text.Rotherham: But, if, a woman, have long hair, it is a glory to her, for, her long hair, instead of a veil, hath been given to her.
text.KJV: But if a woman have long hair, it is a glory to her: for her hair is given her for a covering.
text.NABRE: whereas if a woman has long hair it is her glory, because long hair has been given [her] for a covering?
text.DRB: but if a woman nourish her hair, it is a glory for her, because hair is given her for a veil?

text.NRSV: If anyone is disposed to be contentious—we have no such custom, nor do the churches of God.
text.Rotherham: But, if anyone thinketh to be contentious, we, have no such custom,—nor yet the assemblies of God.
text.KJV: But if any man seem to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither the churches of God.
text.NABRE: But if anyone is inclined to be argumentative, we do not have such a custom, nor do the churches of God.
text.DRB: But if any man seem to be contentious, we have no such custom, nor the Church of God.

text.NRSV: Now in the following instructions I do not commend you, because when you come together it is not for the better but for the worse.
text.Rotherham: But, in giving you the following charge, I praise you not,—in that, not for the better, but for the worse, ye come together.
text.KJV: Now in this that I declare unto you I praise you not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.
text.NABRE: B. The Lord’s Supper An Abuse at Corinth. In giving this instruction, I do not praise the fact that your meetings are doing more harm than good.
text.DRB: And this I command: not praising it, that you come together not to better, but to worse.

text.NRSV: For, to begin with, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you; and to some extent I believe it.
text.Rotherham: For, first of all,—when ye come together in assembly, I hear that divisions among you exist,—and, in part, I believe it;
text.KJV: For first of all, when ye come together in the church, I hear that there be divisions among you; and I partly believe it.
text.NABRE: First of all, I hear that when you meet as a church there are divisions among you, and to a degree I believe it;
text.DRB: First indeed when you come together into the Church, I hear that there are schisms among you, and in part I believe it.

text.NRSV: Indeed, there have to be factions among you, for only so will it become clear who among you are genuine.
text.Rotherham: For there must needs be, even parties among you, that, the approved, [themselves] may become manifest among you.
text.KJV: For there must be also heresies among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.
text.NABRE: there have to be factions among you in order that [also] those who are approved among you may become known.
text.DRB: For there must be heresies also: that they also which are approved, may be made manifest among you.

text.NRSV: When you come together, it is not really to eat the Lord’s supper.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, ye come together into one place, it is not to eat, a supper unto the Lord;
text.KJV: When ye come together therefore into one place, this is not to eat the Lord’s supper.
text.NABRE: When you meet in one place, then, it is not to eat the Lord’s supper,
text.DRB: When you come therefore together in one, it is not now to eat our Lord's supper.

text.NRSV: For when the time comes to eat, each of you goes ahead with your own supper, and one goes hungry and another becomes drunk.
text.Rotherham: For, each one, taketh beforehand, his own supper, in your eating, and, one, indeed, is hungry, whereas, another, is drinking to excess.
text.KJV: For in eating every one taketh before other his own supper: and one is hungry, and another is drunken.
text.NABRE: for in eating, each one goes ahead with his own supper, and one goes hungry while another gets drunk.
text.DRB: For every one taketh his own supper before to eat. And one certes is an hungered, and another is drunk.

text.NRSV: What! Do you not have homes to eat and drink in? Or do you show contempt for the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What should I say to you? Should I commend you? In this matter I do not commend you!
text.Rotherham: What! have ye not houses for your eating and drinking? or, the assembly of God, do ye despise, and, put to shame them who have nothing? What am I to say to you? Shall I praise you? In this, I praise you not.
text.KJV: What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the church of God, and shame them that have not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise you not.
text.NABRE: Do you not have houses in which you can eat and drink? Or do you show contempt for the church of God and make those who have nothing feel ashamed? What can I say to you? Shall I praise you? In this matter I do not praise you.
text.DRB: Why, have you not houses to eat and drink in? or contemn ye the Church of God: and confound them that have not? What shall I say to you? praise I you in this? I do not praise you.

text.NRSV: For I received from the Lord what I also handed on to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night when he was betrayed took a loaf of bread,
text.Rotherham: For, I myself, received from the Lord—that which I also delivered unto you,—how that, the Lord Jesus, in the night in which he was being delivered up, took a loaf,
text.KJV: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, That the Lord Jesus the same night in which he was betrayed took bread:
text.NABRE: Tradition of the Institution. For I received from the Lord what I also handed on to you, that the Lord Jesus, on the night he was handed over, took bread,
text.DRB: For I received of our Lord that which also I have delivered unto you, that our Lord Jesus in the night that he was betrayed, took bread:

text.NRSV: and when he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body that is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
text.Rotherham: And, giving thanks, brake it, and said—This, is my body, which is for you,—this, do ye, in remembrance of me;
text.KJV: And when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.
text.NABRE: and, after he had given thanks, broke it and said, “This is my body that is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
text.DRB: and giving thanks brake, and said: Take ye & eat, This is my Body Which Shall be Delivered For You. This do ye for the commemoration of me.

text.NRSV: In the same way he took the cup also, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
text.Rotherham: In the same manner, the cup also, after they had taken supper, saying—This cup, is, the new covenant in my blood, this, do ye, as often soever as ye may be drinking it, in remembrance of me.
text.KJV: After the same manner also he took the cup, when he had supped, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink it, in remembrance of me.
text.NABRE: In the same way also the cup, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
text.DRB: In like manner also the chalice after he had supped, saying: This Chalice is The New Testament in my Blood. This do ye, as often as you shall drink, for the commemoration of me.

text.NRSV: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.
text.Rotherham: For, as often as ye may be eating this loaf, and, the cup, may be drinking, The death of the Lord, do ye announce, until he come.
text.KJV: For as often as ye eat this bread, and drink this cup, ye do shew the Lord’s death till he come.
text.NABRE: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the death of the Lord until he comes.
text.DRB: For as often as you shall eat this bread, and drink the chalice, you shall shew the death of our Lord, until he come.

text.NRSV: Whoever, therefore, eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be answerable for the body and blood of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So that, whosoever may be eating the loaf, or drinking the cup of the Lord, in an unworthy manner, shall be responsible for the body and blood of the Lord.
text.KJV: Wherefore whosoever shall eat this bread, and drink this cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Therefore whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord unworthily will have to answer for the body and blood of the Lord.
text.DRB: Therefore whosoever shall eat this bread, or drink the chalice of our Lord unworthily, he shall be guilty of the body and of the blood of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Examine yourselves, and only then eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
text.Rotherham: But let a man be proving himself,—and, so, of the loaf, let him eat, and, of the cup, let him drink;
text.KJV: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of that bread, and drink of that cup.
text.NABRE: A person should examine himself, and so eat the bread and drink the cup.
text.DRB: But let a man prove himself: and so, let him eat of that bread, and drink of the chalice.

text.NRSV: For all who eat and drink without discerning the body, eat and drink judgment against themselves.
text.Rotherham: For, he that is eating and drinking,—judgment unto himself, doth eat and drink,—if he be not setting apart the body.
text.KJV: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation to himself, not discerning the Lord’s body.
text.NABRE: For anyone who eats and drinks without discerning the body, eats and drinks judgment on himself.
text.DRB: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh judgement to himself, not discerning the body of our Lord.

text.NRSV: For this reason many of you are weak and ill, and some have died.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, many among you are weak and sickly; and, not a few, are falling asleep.
text.KJV: For this cause many are weak and sickly among you, and many sleep.
text.NABRE: That is why many among you are ill and infirm, and a considerable number are dying.
text.DRB: Therefore are there among you many weak and feeble, and many sleep.

text.NRSV: But if we judged ourselves, we would not be judged.
text.Rotherham: If, however, we had been setting, ourselves, apart, we had not, in that case, been coming under judgment;
text.KJV: For if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
text.NABRE: If we discerned ourselves, we would not be under judgment;
text.DRB: But if we did judge ourselves, we should not be judged.

text.NRSV: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined so that we may not be condemned along with the world.
text.Rotherham: But, being brought under judgment, by the Lord, are we being disciplined, lest, with the world, we should be condemned.
text.KJV: But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
text.NABRE: but since we are judged by [the] Lord, we are being disciplined so that we may not be condemned along with the world.
text.DRB: But whiles we are judged, of our Lord we are chastised; that with this world we be not damned.

text.NRSV: So then, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
text.Rotherham: So then, my brethren,—when ye come together for the eating, unto one another, be giving welcome:—
text.KJV: Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.
text.NABRE: Therefore, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
text.DRB: Therefore, my brethren, when you come together to eat, expect one another.

text.NRSV: If you are hungry, eat at home, so that when you come together, it will not be for your condemnation. About the other things I will give instructions when I come.
text.Rotherham: If anyone be hungry, at home, let him eat, lest, unto judgment, ye be coming together. And, the remaining things, whensoever I come, I will set in order.
text.KJV: And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.
text.NABRE: If anyone is hungry, he should eat at home, so that your meetings may not result in judgment. The other matters I shall set in order when I come.
text.DRB: If any man be an hungered, let him eat at home; that you come not together unto judgement. And the rest I will dispose, when I come.

text.NRSV: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brothers and sisters, I do not want you to be uninformed.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning the spiritually gifted, brethren, I am not wishing you to be ignorant.
text.KJV: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I would not have you ignorant.
text.NABRE: C. Spiritual Gifts Chapter 12 Unity and Variety. Now in regard to spiritual gifts, brothers, I do not want you to be unaware.
text.DRB: And concerning spiritual things, I will not have you ignorant, brethren.

text.NRSV: You know that when you were pagans, you were enticed and led astray to idols that could not speak.
text.Rotherham: Ye know that, when ye were of the nations, unto the dumb idols, howsoever ye were being led, ye were seduced.
text.KJV: Ye know that ye were Gentiles, carried away unto these dumb idols, even as ye were led.
text.NABRE: You know how, when you were pagans, you were constantly attracted and led away to mute idols.
text.DRB: You know that when you were heathen, you went to dumb idols according as you were led.

text.NRSV: Therefore I want you to understand that no one speaking by the Spirit of God ever says “Let Jesus be cursed!” and no one can say “Jesus is Lord” except by the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, I give you to know—that, no one, in God’s Spirit speaking, saith—Accursed Jesus! and, no one, can say—Lord Jesus! save in [the] Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: Wherefore I give you to understand, that no man speaking by the Spirit of God calleth Jesus accursed: and that no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, but by the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I tell you that nobody speaking by the spirit of God says, “Jesus be accursed.” And no one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” except by the holy Spirit.
text.DRB: Therefore I do you to understand that no man speaking in the Spirit of God, saith anathema to Jesus. And no man can say, Our Lord Jesus, but in the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit;
text.Rotherham: But, distributions of gifts, there are, yet the same Spirit,
text.KJV: Now there are diversities of gifts, but the same Spirit.
text.NABRE: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts but the same Spirit;
text.DRB: And there are divisions of graces, but one Spirit.

text.NRSV: and there are varieties of services, but the same Lord;
text.Rotherham: And, distributions of ministries, there are, and the same Lord,
text.KJV: And there are differences of administrations, but the same Lord.
text.NABRE: there are different forms of service but the same Lord;
text.DRB: And there are divisions of ministrations; but one Lord.

text.NRSV: and there are varieties of activities, but it is the same God who activates all of them in everyone.
text.Rotherham: And, distributions of energies, there are, and the same God—who energiseth all things in all.
text.KJV: And there are diversities of operations, but it is the same God which worketh all in all.
text.NABRE: there are different workings but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
text.DRB: And there are divisions of operations, but one God, which worketh all in all.

text.NRSV: To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
text.Rotherham: But, unto each one, is given the manifesting of the Spirit, with a view to that which is profitable;
text.KJV: But the manifestation of the Spirit is given to every man to profit withal.
text.NABRE: To each individual the manifestation of the Spirit is given for some benefit.
text.DRB: And the manifestation of the Spirit is given unto every one to profit.

text.NRSV: To one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom, and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit,
text.Rotherham: For, unto one, indeed, through the Spirit, is given, a word of wisdom, but, unto another, a word of knowledge, according to the same Spirit;—
text.KJV: For to one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom; to another the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;
text.NABRE: To one is given through the Spirit the expression of wisdom; to another the expression of knowledge according to the same Spirit;
text.DRB: To one certes by the Spirit is given the word of wisdom: and to another, the word of knowledge according to the same Spirit:

text.NRSV: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit,
text.Rotherham: Unto a different one, faith, in the same Spirit, and, unto another, gifts of healings, in the one Spirit,
text.KJV: To another faith by the same Spirit; to another the gifts of healing by the same Spirit;
text.NABRE: to another faith by the same Spirit; to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit;
text.DRB: to another, faith in the same Spirit: to another, the grace of doing cures in one Spirit:

text.NRSV: to another the working of miracles, to another prophecy, to another the discernment of spirits, to another various kinds of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues.
text.Rotherham: And, unto another, energies of mighty works, [and], unto another, prophesying, [and], unto another, discriminations of spirits,—unto a different one, kinds of tongues, and, unto another, translation of tongues;—
text.KJV: To another the working of miracles; to another prophecy; to another discerning of spirits; to another divers kinds of tongues; to another the interpretation of tongues:
text.NABRE: to another mighty deeds; to another prophecy; to another discernment of spirits; to another varieties of tongues; to another interpretation of tongues.
text.DRB: to another, the working of miracles: to another, prophecy: to another, discerning of spirits: to another, kinds of tongues: to another, interpretation of languages.

text.NRSV: All these are activated by one and the same Spirit, who allots to each one individually just as the Spirit chooses.
text.Rotherham: But, all these, energiseth the one and the same Spirit, distributing unto each one, peculiarly, even as it is disposed.
text.KJV: But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit, dividing to every man severally as he will.
text.NABRE: But one and the same Spirit produces all of these, distributing them individually to each person as he wishes. One Body, Many Parts.
text.DRB: And all these things worketh one and the same Spirit, dividing to every one according as he will.

text.NRSV: For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so it is with Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, just as, the body, is one, and yet hath many members, but, all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so, also, the Christ;—
text.KJV: For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also is Christ.
text.NABRE: As a body is one though it has many parts, and all the parts of the body, though many, are one body, so also Christ.
text.DRB: For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of the body whereas they be many, yet are one body; so also Christ.

text.NRSV: For in the one Spirit we were all baptized into one body—Jews or Greeks, slaves or free—and we were all made to drink of one Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For, even to one Spirit, we all, into one body, have been immersed,—whether Jews or Greeks, whether bond or free,—and, all, of one Spirit, have been caused to drink.
text.KJV: For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body, whether we be Jews or Gentiles, whether we be bond or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.
text.NABRE: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, slaves or free persons, and we were all given to drink of one Spirit.
text.DRB: For in one Spirit were we all baptized into one, whether Jews, or Gentiles, or bondmen, or free: & in one Spirit we were all made to drink.

text.NRSV: Indeed, the body does not consist of one member but of many.
text.Rotherham: For, the body also, is not one member, but many:
text.KJV: For the body is not one member, but many.
text.NABRE: Now the body is not a single part, but many.
text.DRB: For the body also is not one member, but many.

text.NRSV: If the foot would say, “Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
text.Rotherham: Though the foot should say—Because I am not a hand, I am not of the body, Is it, therefore, not, of the body?
text.KJV: If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
text.NABRE: If a foot should say, “Because I am not a hand I do not belong to the body,” it does not for this reason belong any less to the body.
text.DRB: If the foot should say, because I am not the hand, I am not of the body: is it therefore not of the body?

text.NRSV: And if the ear would say, “Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
text.Rotherham: And, though the ear should say—Because I am not an eye, I am not of the body, Is it, therefore, not, of the body?
text.KJV: And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
text.NABRE: Or if an ear should say, “Because I am not an eye I do not belong to the body,” it does not for this reason belong any less to the body.
text.DRB: And if the ear should say, because I am not the eye, I am not of the body: is he therefore not of the body?

text.NRSV: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole body were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?
text.Rotherham: If, the whole body, were, an eye, where were the hearing? If, the whole were, hearing, where were, the smelling?
text.KJV: If the whole body were an eye, where were the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where were the smelling?
text.NABRE: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole body were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?
text.DRB: If the whole body were the eye: where is the hearing? If the whole were the hearing: where is the smelling?

text.NRSV: But as it is, God arranged the members in the body, each one of them, as he chose.
text.Rotherham: But, now, hath God set the members, each one of them in the body, even as he pleased.
text.KJV: But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.
text.NABRE: But as it is, God placed the parts, each one of them, in the body as he intended.
text.DRB: But now God hath set the members, every one of them in the body as he would.

text.NRSV: If all were a single member, where would the body be?
text.Rotherham: But, if they all had been one member, Where had been, the body?
text.KJV: And if they were all one member, where were the body?
text.NABRE: If they were all one part, where would the body be?
text.DRB: And if all were one member, where were the body?

text.NRSV: As it is, there are many members, yet one body.
text.Rotherham: Now, however there are many members, yet one body;
text.KJV: But now are they many members, yet but one body.
text.NABRE: But as it is, there are many parts, yet one body.
text.DRB: But now there are many members indeed, yet one body.

text.NRSV: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
text.Rotherham: [And] the eye cannot say unto the hand—I have no need of thee,—nor, again, the head, unto the feet—I have no need of you:—
text.KJV: And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the head to the feet, I have no need of you.
text.NABRE: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I do not need you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I do not need you.”
text.DRB: And the eye cannot say to the hand: I need not thy help; or again the head to the feet: You are not necessary for me.

text.NRSV: On the contrary, the members of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
text.Rotherham: Nay! much more, the members which seem to be weaker are necessary,
text.KJV: Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be more feeble, are necessary:
text.NABRE: Indeed, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are all the more necessary,
text.DRB: But much more those that seem to be the more weak members of the body, are more necessary:

text.NRSV: and those members of the body that we think less honorable we clothe with greater honor, and our less respectable members are treated with greater respect;
text.Rotherham: And, the parts of the body which we deem to be less honourable, on these, more abundant honour, do we bestow, and, our uncomely parts, have, more abundant comeliness,
text.KJV: And those members of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness.
text.NABRE: and those parts of the body that we consider less honorable we surround with greater honor, and our less presentable parts are treated with greater propriety,
text.DRB: and such as we think to be the baser members of the body, upon them we put more aboundant honour: and those that are our unhonest parts, have more aboundant honesty.

text.NRSV: whereas our more respectable members do not need this. But God has so constructed the body, giving the greater honor to the inferior member,
text.Rotherham: Whereas, our comely parts, have, no need. But, God, hath tempered the body together,—unto that which was lacking, giving more abundant honour;—
text.KJV: For our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that part which lacked:
text.NABRE: whereas our more presentable parts do not need this. But God has so constructed the body as to give greater honor to a part that is without it,
text.DRB: And our honest parts need nothing: but God hath tempered the body, giving to it that wanted, the more aboundant honour,

text.NRSV: so that there may be no dissension in the body, but the members may have the same care for one another.
text.Rotherham: That there might be no division in the body, but that the members might have, the same, care, one for another;
text.KJV: That there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same care one for another.
text.NABRE: so that there may be no division in the body, but that the parts may have the same concern for one another.
text.DRB: that there might be no schism in the body, but the members together might be careful one for another.

text.NRSV: If one member suffers, all suffer together with it; if one member is honored, all rejoice together with it.
text.Rotherham: And, whether one member, suffereth, all the members, suffer with it, or, a member is glorified, all the members, rejoice together.
text.KJV: And whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.
text.NABRE: If [one] part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part is honored, all the parts share its joy. Application to Christ.
text.DRB: And if one member suffer any thing, all the members suffer with it. Or if one member do glory, all the members rejoice with it.

text.NRSV: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.
text.Rotherham: Now, ye, are the body of Christ, and members severally;—
text.KJV: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.
text.NABRE: Now you are Christ’s body, and individually parts of it.
text.DRB: And you are the body of Christ, and members of member.

text.NRSV: And God has appointed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers; then deeds of power, then gifts of healing, forms of assistance, forms of leadership, various kinds of tongues.
text.Rotherham: And God hath, indeed, set, certain, in the assembly,—first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, after that mighty works, then gifts of healings, helps, guidings, kinds of tongues.
text.KJV: And God hath set some in the church, first apostles, secondarily prophets, thirdly teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, diversities of tongues.
text.NABRE: Some people God has designated in the church to be, first, apostles; second, prophets; third, teachers; then, mighty deeds; then, gifts of healing, assistance, administration, and varieties of tongues.
text.DRB: And some verily God hath set in the Church first Apostles, secondly Prophets, thirdly Doctors, next miracles, then the graces of doing cures, helps, governments, kinds of tongues.

text.NRSV: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
text.Rotherham: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all mighty works?
text.KJV: Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of miracles?
text.NABRE: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work mighty deeds?
text.DRB: Are all Apostles? are all Prophets? are all Doctors?

text.NRSV: Do all possess gifts of healing? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?
text.Rotherham: Have, all, gifts of healings? Do, all, with tongues, speak? Do, all, translate?
text.KJV: Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
text.NABRE: Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?
text.DRB: are all miracles? have all the grace of doing cures? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?

text.NRSV: But strive for the greater gifts. And I will show you a still more excellent way.
text.Rotherham: Be envious, however, of the greater gifts; and, yet, a much more excellent way, unto you, I point out:—
text.KJV: But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.
text.NABRE: Strive eagerly for the greatest spiritual gifts. The Way of Love. But I shall show you a still more excellent way.
text.DRB: But pursue the better gifts. And yet I shew you a more excellent way.

text.NRSV: If I speak in the tongues of mortals and of angels, but do not have love, I am a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
text.Rotherham: Although with the tongues of men, I be speaking, and of messengers, and have not, love, I have become resounding brass, or a clanging cymbal;
text.KJV: Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 If I speak in human and angelic tongues but do not have love, I am a resounding gong or a clashing cymbal.
text.DRB: IF I speak with the tongues of men, and of Angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.

text.NRSV: And if I have prophetic powers, and understand all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but do not have love, I am nothing.
text.Rotherham: And, though I have [the gift of] prophesying, and know all sacred secrets, and all knowledge,—and though I have all faith, so as to be removing mountains, and have not, love, I am, nothing;
text.KJV: And though I have the gift of prophecy, and understand all mysteries, and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.
text.NABRE: And if I have the gift of prophecy and comprehend all mysteries and all knowledge; if I have all faith so as to move mountains but do not have love, I am nothing.
text.DRB: And if I should have prophecy, and knew all mysteries, and all knowledge, & if I should have all faith so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.

text.NRSV: If I give away all my possessions, and if I hand over my body so that I may boast, but do not have love, I gain nothing.
text.Rotherham: And, though I morsel out all my goods,—and though I deliver up my body, that I may boast, and have not, love, I am profited, nothing.
text.KJV: And though I bestow all my goods to feed the poor, and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.
text.NABRE: If I give away everything I own, and if I hand my body over so that I may boast but do not have love, I gain nothing.
text.DRB: And if I should distribute all my goods to be meat for the poor, and if I should deliver my body so that I burn, and have not charity, it doth profit me nothing.

text.NRSV: Love is patient; love is kind; love is not envious or boastful or arrogant
text.Rotherham: Love, is patient, is gracious. Love, is not envious, vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,
text.KJV: Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,
text.NABRE: Love is patient, love is kind. It is not jealous, [love] is not pompous, it is not inflated,
text.DRB: Charity is patient, is benign: Charity envieth not, dealeth not perversely: is not puffed up,

text.NRSV: or rude. It does not insist on its own way; it is not irritable or resentful;
text.Rotherham: Acteth not unbecomingly, seeketh not her own things, is not easily provoked, imputeth not that which is base,
text.KJV: Doth not behave itself unseemly, seeketh not her own, is not easily provoked, thinketh no evil;
text.NABRE: it is not rude, it does not seek its own interests, it is not quick-tempered, it does not brood over injury,
text.DRB: is not ambitious, seeketh not her own, is not provoked to anger, thinketh not evil,

text.NRSV: it does not rejoice in wrongdoing, but rejoices in the truth.
text.Rotherham: Rejoiceth not over unrighteousness, but rejoiceth in sympathy with truth,—
text.KJV: Rejoiceth not in iniquity, but rejoiceth in the truth;
text.NABRE: it does not rejoice over wrongdoing but rejoices with the truth.
text.DRB: rejoiceth not upon iniquity, but rejoiceth with the truth:

text.NRSV: It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
text.Rotherham: All things, covereth, all things, believeth, all things, hopeth, all things, endureth.
text.KJV: Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.
text.NABRE: It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
text.DRB: suffereth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, beareth all things.

text.NRSV: Love never ends. But as for prophecies, they will come to an end; as for tongues, they will cease; as for knowledge, it will come to an end.
text.Rotherham: Love, at no time, faileth;—but, whether prophesyings, they shall be done away, whether tongues, they shall cease, whether gaining knowledge, it shall be done away;
text.KJV: Charity never faileth: but whether there be prophecies, they shall fail; whether there be tongues, they shall cease; whether there be knowledge, it shall vanish away.
text.NABRE: Love never fails. If there are prophecies, they will be brought to nothing; if tongues, they will cease; if knowledge, it will be brought to nothing.
text.DRB: Charity never falleth away: whether prophecies shall be made void, or tongues shall cease, or knowledge shall be destroyed.

text.NRSV: For we know only in part, and we prophesy only in part;
text.Rotherham: For, in part, are we gaining knowledge, and, in part, are we prophesying,—
text.KJV: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part.
text.NABRE: For we know partially and we prophesy partially,
text.DRB: For in part we know, & in part we prophesy.

text.NRSV: but when the complete comes, the partial will come to an end.
text.Rotherham: But, as soon as that which is complete is come, that which is in part, shall be done away.
text.KJV: But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away.
text.NABRE: but when the perfect comes, the partial will pass away.
text.DRB: But when that shall come that is perfect, that shall be made void that is in part.

text.NRSV: When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child; when I became an adult, I put an end to childish ways.
text.Rotherham: When I was a child, I used to speak as a child, to prefer as child, to reason as a child: now I have become a man, I have laid aside the things of the child!
text.KJV: When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.
text.NABRE: When I was a child, I used to talk as a child, think as a child, reason as a child; when I became a man, I put aside childish things.
text.DRB: When I was a little one, I spake as a little one, I understood as a little one, I thought as a little one. But when I was made a man, I did away the things that belonged to a little one.

text.NRSV: For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then we will see face to face. Now I know only in part; then I will know fully, even as I have been fully known.
text.Rotherham: For we see, as yet, through a dim window, obscurely, but, then, face to face: as yet, I gain knowledge, in part, but, then, shall I fully know, even as I was also fully known.
text.KJV: For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.
text.NABRE: At present we see indistinctly, as in a mirror, but then face to face. At present I know partially; then I shall know fully, as I am fully known.
text.DRB: We see now by a glass in a dark sort: but then face to face. Now I know in part: but then I shall know as also I am known.

text.NRSV: And now faith, hope, and love abide, these three; and the greatest of these is love.
text.Rotherham: But, now abide—faith, hope, love,—these three; but, the greatest of these, is, love.
text.KJV: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
text.NABRE: So faith, hope, love remain, these three; but the greatest of these is love.
text.DRB: And now there remain, faith, hope, charity, these three: but the greater of these is charity.

text.NRSV: Pursue love and strive for the spiritual gifts, and especially that you may prophesy.
text.Rotherham: Pursue love; nevertheless be envious of the spiritual gifts,—and, rather, that ye may be prophesying.
text.KJV: Follow after charity, and desire spiritual gifts, but rather that ye may prophesy.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 Prophecy Greater than Tongues. Pursue love, but strive eagerly for the spiritual gifts, above all that you may prophesy.
text.DRB: Follow Charity, earnestly pursue spiritual things: but rather that you may prophesy.

text.NRSV: For those who speak in a tongue do not speak to other people but to God; for no one understands them, since they are speaking mysteries in the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For, he that speaketh with a tongue, not unto men, doth speak, but, unto God,—for, no one, understandeth, although, in spirit, he is speaking sacred secrets;
text.KJV: For he that speaketh in an unknown tongue speaketh not unto men, but unto God: for no man understandeth him; howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries.
text.NABRE: For one who speaks in a tongue does not speak to human beings but to God, for no one listens; he utters mysteries in spirit.
text.DRB: For he that speaketh with tongues speaketh not to men, but to God: for no man heareth. But in spirit he speaketh mysteries.

text.NRSV: On the other hand, those who prophesy speak to other people for their upbuilding and encouragement and consolation.
text.Rotherham: But, be that prophesieth, unto men, doth speak—edification, and exhortation, and comfort.
text.KJV: But he that prophesieth speaketh unto men to edification, and exhortation, and comfort.
text.NABRE: On the other hand, one who prophesies does speak to human beings, for their building up, encouragement, and solace.
text.DRB: For he that prophesieth, speaketh to men unto edification, & exhortation, & consolation.

text.NRSV: Those who speak in a tongue build up themselves, but those who prophesy build up the church.
text.Rotherham: He that speaketh with a tongue, buildeth up, himself, whereas, he that prophesieth, buildeth up, an assembly.
text.KJV: He that speaketh in an unknown tongue edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth edifieth the church.
text.NABRE: Whoever speaks in a tongue builds himself up, but whoever prophesies builds up the church.
text.DRB: He that speaketh with tongues, edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth, edifieth the Church.

text.NRSV: Now I would like all of you to speak in tongues, but even more to prophesy. One who prophesies is greater than one who speaks in tongues, unless someone interprets, so that the church may be built up.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I wish you all to speak with tongues, but, rather, that ye may prophesy,—moreover, greater, is he that prophesieth, than he that speaketh with tongues,—unless indeed he translate, that, the assembly, may receive upbuilding.
text.KJV: I would that ye all spake with tongues, but rather that ye prophesied: for greater is he that prophesieth than he that speaketh with tongues, except he interpret, that the church may receive edifying.
text.NABRE: Now I should like all of you to speak in tongues, but even more to prophesy. One who prophesies is greater than one who speaks in tongues, unless he interprets, so that the church may be built up.
text.DRB: And I would have you all to speak with tongues, but rather to prophesy. For greater is he that prophesieth, than he that speaketh with tongues: unless perhaps he interpret, that the Church may take edification.

text.NRSV: Now, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I benefit you unless I speak to you in some revelation or knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
text.Rotherham: But, now, brethren—if I come unto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit, you, except I speak, unto you, either by way of revelation, or knowledge, or prophesying, or teaching?
text.KJV: Now, brethren, if I come unto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, except I shall speak to you either by revelation, or by knowledge, or by prophesying, or by doctrine?
text.NABRE: Now, brothers, if I should come to you speaking in tongues, what good will I do you if I do not speak to you by way of revelation, or knowledge, or prophecy, or instruction?
text.DRB: But now, brethren; if I come to you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, unless I speak to you either in revelation, or in knowledge, or in prophecy, or in doctrine?

text.NRSV: It is the same way with lifeless instruments that produce sound, such as the flute or the harp. If they do not give distinct notes, how will anyone know what is being played?
text.Rotherham: In like manner, the things without life giving sound, whether pipe or harp, if, a distinction in the sounds, they do not give, how shall it be known, what is being piped or harped?
text.KJV: And even things without life giving sound, whether pipe or harp, except they give a distinction in the sounds, how shall it be known what is piped or harped?
text.NABRE: Likewise, if inanimate things that produce sound, such as flute or harp, do not give out the tones distinctly, how will what is being played on flute or harp be recognized?
text.DRB: Yet the things without life that give a sound, be it pipe or harp, unless they give a distinction of sounds, how shall that be known which is piped, or which is harped?

text.NRSV: And if the bugle gives an indistinct sound, who will get ready for battle?
text.Rotherham: For, if also, an uncertain sound, a trumpet, should give, who shall prepare himself for battle?
text.KJV: For if the trumpet give an uncertain sound, who shall prepare himself to the battle?
text.NABRE: And if the bugle gives an indistinct sound, who will get ready for battle?
text.DRB: For if the trumpet give an uncertain voice, who shall prepare himself to battle?

text.NRSV: So with yourselves; if in a tongue you utter speech that is not intelligible, how will anyone know what is being said? For you will be speaking into the air.
text.Rotherham: So, also, ye, through means of the tongue, except ye give intelligible discourse, how shall it be known what is being spoken? for ye will be speaking, to the air.
text.KJV: So likewise ye, except ye utter by the tongue words easy to be understood, how shall it be known what is spoken? for ye shall speak into the air.
text.NABRE: Similarly, if you, because of speaking in tongues, do not utter intelligible speech, how will anyone know what is being said? For you will be talking to the air.
text.DRB: So you also by a tongue unless you utter manifest speech, how shall that be known that is said? for you shall be speaking into the air.

text.NRSV: There are doubtless many different kinds of sounds in the world, and nothing is without sound.
text.Rotherham: There may happen to be so many kinds of languages in the world, and, not one, unspoken:—
text.KJV: There are, it may be, so many kinds of voices in the world, and none of them is without signification.
text.NABRE: It happens that there are many different languages in the world, and none is meaningless;
text.DRB: There are (for example) so many kinds of tongues in this world, & none is without voice.

text.NRSV: If then I do not know the meaning of a sound, I will be a foreigner to the speaker and the speaker a foreigner to me.
text.Rotherham: If, then, I do not know the meaning of the language, I shall be, unto him that is speaking, a foreigner, and, he that is speaking, shall be, in my case, a foreigner.
text.KJV: Therefore if I know not the meaning of the voice, I shall be unto him that speaketh a barbarian, and he that speaketh shall be a barbarian unto me.
text.NABRE: but if I do not know the meaning of a language, I shall be a foreigner to one who speaks it, and one who speaks it a foreigner to me.
text.DRB: If then I know not the virtue of the voice, I shall be to him to whom I speak, barbarous; and he that speaketh barbarous to me.

text.NRSV: So with yourselves; since you are eager for spiritual gifts, strive to excel in them for building up the church.
text.Rotherham: So, ye, also—since ye are, envious, of spirits, unto the upbuilding of the assembly, seek to be pre-eminent.
text.KJV: Even so ye, forasmuch as ye are zealous of spiritual gifts, seek that ye may excel to the edifying of the church.
text.NABRE: So with yourselves: since you strive eagerly for spirits, seek to have an abundance of them for building up the church. Need for Interpretation.
text.DRB: So you also, because you be emulators of spirits: seek to abound unto the edifying of the Church.

text.NRSV: Therefore, one who speaks in a tongue should pray for the power to interpret.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, he that speaketh with a tongue, let him pray that he may translate;
text.KJV: Wherefore let him that speaketh in an unknown tongue pray that he may interpret.
text.NABRE: Therefore, one who speaks in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
text.DRB: And therefore he that speaketh with the tongue, let him pray that he may interpret.

text.NRSV: For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind is unproductive.
text.Rotherham: [For], if I am praying in a tongue, my spirit, is praying, but, my mind, is unfruitful.
text.KJV: For if I pray in an unknown tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my understanding is unfruitful.
text.NABRE: [For] if I pray in a tongue, my spirit is at prayer but my mind is unproductive.
text.DRB: For if I pray with the tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my understanding is without fruit.

text.NRSV: What should I do then? I will pray with the spirit, but I will pray with the mind also; I will sing praise with the spirit, but I will sing praise with the mind also.
text.Rotherham: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, but I will pray also with the mind,—I will strike the strings with the spirit, [but] I will strike the strings also with the mind.
text.KJV: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also: I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.
text.NABRE: So what is to be done? I will pray with the spirit, but I will also pray with the mind. I will sing praise with the spirit, but I will also sing praise with the mind.
text.DRB: What is it then? I will pray in the spirit, I will pray also in the understanding: I will sing in the spirit, I will sing also in the understanding.

text.NRSV: Otherwise, if you say a blessing with the spirit, how can anyone in the position of an outsider say the “Amen” to your thanksgiving, since the outsider does not know what you are saying?
text.Rotherham: Else, if thou be blessing in a spirit, he that filleth up the place of the ungifted person, how shall he say the Amen upon thy thanksgiving? since indeed, what thou art saying, he knoweth not;
text.KJV: Else when thou shalt bless with the spirit, how shall he that occupieth the room of the unlearned say Amen at thy giving of thanks, seeing he understandeth not what thou sayest?
text.NABRE: Otherwise, if you pronounce a blessing [with] the spirit, how shall one who holds the place of the uninstructed say the “Amen” to your thanksgiving, since he does not know what you are saying?
text.DRB: But if thou bless in the spirit, he that supplieth the place of the vulgar how shall he say, Amen, upon thy blessing? because he knoweth not what thou sayest.

text.NRSV: For you may give thanks well enough, but the other person is not built up.
text.Rotherham: For, thou, indeed, excellently art giving thanks, but, the other, is not being built up.
text.KJV: For thou verily givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.
text.NABRE: For you may be giving thanks very well, but the other is not built up.
text.DRB: For thou indeed givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.

text.NRSV: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you;
text.Rotherham: I give thanks unto God!—More than ye all, am I speaking with tongues;
text.KJV: I thank my God, I speak with tongues more than ye all:
text.NABRE: I give thanks to God that I speak in tongues more than any of you,
text.DRB: I give my God thanks, that I speak with the tongue of you all.

text.NRSV: nevertheless, in church I would rather speak five words with my mind, in order to instruct others, than ten thousand words in a tongue.
text.Rotherham: But, in assembly, I desire to speak five words with my mind, that, others also, I may instruct, than myriads of words in a tongue.
text.KJV: Yet in the church I had rather speak five words with my understanding, that by my voice I might teach others also, than ten thousand words in an unknown tongue.
text.NABRE: but in the church I would rather speak five words with my mind, so as to instruct others also, than ten thousand words in a tongue.
text.DRB: But in the Church I will speak five words with my understanding that I may instruct others also; rather than ten thousand words in a tongue.

text.NRSV: Brothers and sisters, do not be children in your thinking; rather, be infants in evil, but in thinking be adults.
text.Rotherham: Brethren! do not become children, in your understandings; but, in baseness, become babes, while, in your understandings, ye become, full-grown.
text.KJV: Brethren, be not children in understanding: howbeit in malice be ye children, but in understanding be men.
text.NABRE: Functions of These Gifts. Brothers, stop being childish in your thinking. In respect to evil be like infants, but in your thinking be mature.
text.DRB: Brethren, be not made children in sense, but in malice be children, and in sense be perfect.

text.NRSV: In the law it is written, “By people of strange tongues and by the lips of foreigners I will speak to this people; yet even then they will not listen to me,” says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: In the law, it is written—With strange tongues, and with lips of strangers, will I speak unto this people; and, not even so, will they hearken unto me,—saith the Lord.
text.KJV: In the law it is written, With men of other tongues and other lips will I speak unto this people; and yet for all that will they not hear me, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: It is written in the law: “By people speaking strange tongues and by the lips of foreigners I will speak to this people, and even so they will not listen to me, says the Lord.”
text.DRB: In the Law it is written: That in other tongues and other lips I will speak to this people: and neither so will they hear me, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Tongues, then, are a sign not for believers but for unbelievers, while prophecy is not for unbelievers but for believers.
text.Rotherham: So that, the tongues, are for a sign—not unto them that believe, but, unto them that believe not; whereas, prophesying, is not for them that believe not, but, for them that believe.
text.KJV: Wherefore tongues are for a sign, not to them that believe, but to them that believe not: but prophesying serveth not for them that believe not, but for them which believe.
text.NABRE: Thus, tongues are a sign not for those who believe but for unbelievers, whereas prophecy is not for unbelievers but for those who believe.
text.DRB: Therefore languages are for a sign not to the faithful, but to infidels: but prophecies, not to infidels, but to the faithful.

text.NRSV: If, therefore, the whole church comes together and all speak in tongues, and outsiders or unbelievers enter, will they not say that you are out of your mind?
text.Rotherham: If, then, the whole assembly come together with one consent, and, all, are speaking with tongues,—and there come in persons unskilled or unbelieving, will they not say that ye are raving?
text.KJV: If therefore the whole church be come together into one place, and all speak with tongues, and there come in those that are unlearned, or unbelievers, will they not say that ye are mad?
text.NABRE: So if the whole church meets in one place and everyone speaks in tongues, and then uninstructed people or unbelievers should come in, will they not say that you are out of your minds?
text.DRB: If therefore the whole Church come together in one, and all speak with tongues, and there enter in vulgar persons or infidels, will they not say that you be mad?

text.NRSV: But if all prophesy, an unbeliever or outsider who enters is reproved by all and called to account by all.
text.Rotherham: But, if, all, be prophesying,—and there come in one who is unbelieving or unskilled, he is convicted by all, he is searched by all, the secrets of his heart, become manifest,
text.KJV: But if all prophesy, and there come in one that believeth not, or one unlearned, he is convinced of all, he is judged of all:
text.NABRE: But if everyone is prophesying, and an unbeliever or uninstructed person should come in, he will be convinced by everyone and judged by everyone,
text.DRB: But if all prophesy, and there enter in any infidel or vulgar person, he is convinced of all, he is judged of all.

text.NRSV: After the secrets of the unbeliever’s heart are disclosed, that person will bow down before God and worship him, declaring, “God is really among you.”
text.Rotherham: And, so, falling down upon his face, he will do homage unto God, reporting that, in reality, God is, among you.
text.KJV: And thus are the secrets of his heart made manifest; and so falling down on his face he will worship God, and report that God is in you of a truth.
text.NABRE: and the secrets of his heart will be disclosed, and so he will fall down and worship God, declaring, “God is really in your midst.”
text.DRB: the secrets of his heart are made manifest, and so falling on his face he will adore God, pronouncing that God is in you indeed.

text.NRSV: What should be done then, brothers and sisters? When you come together, each one has a hymn, a lesson, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. Let all things be done for building up.
text.Rotherham: What, then, is it, brethren? Whensoever ye are coming together, each one, hath a psalm, hath a teaching, hath a revelation, hath a tongue, hath a translation:—let, all things, be done, unto building up.
text.KJV: How is it then, brethren? when ye come together, every one of you hath a psalm, hath a doctrine, hath a tongue, hath a revelation, hath an interpretation. Let all things be done unto edifying.
text.NABRE: Rules of Order. So what is to be done, brothers? When you assemble, one has a psalm, another an instruction, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. Everything should be done for building up.
text.DRB: What is it then, brethren? when you come together, every one of you hath a psalm, hath a doctrine, hath a revelation, hath a tongue, hath an interpretation: let all things be done to edification.

text.NRSV: If anyone speaks in a tongue, let there be only two or at most three, and each in turn; and let one interpret.
text.Rotherham: If, with a tongue, one is speaking, let it be by two, or, at the most, three,—and by turns; and let, one, be translating;
text.KJV: If any man speak in an unknown tongue, let it be by two, or at the most by three, and that by course; and let one interpret.
text.NABRE: If anyone speaks in a tongue, let it be two or at most three, and each in turn, and one should interpret.
text.DRB: Whether a man speak with tongue, by two, or at the most by three, and in course, and let one interpret.

text.NRSV: But if there is no one to interpret, let them be silent in church and speak to themselves and to God.
text.Rotherham: But, if there be none to translate, let him keep silence in assembly, and, unto himself, be speaking, and, unto God!
text.KJV: But if there be no interpreter, let him keep silence in the church; and let him speak to himself, and to God.
text.NABRE: But if there is no interpreter, the person should keep silent in the church and speak to himself and to God.
text.DRB: But if there be not an interpreter, let him hold his peace in the Church, and speak to himself and to God.

text.NRSV: Let two or three prophets speak, and let the others weigh what is said.
text.Rotherham: Prophets, moreover, let two or three speak, and let, the others, judge.
text.KJV: Let the prophets speak two or three, and let the other judge.
text.NABRE: Two or three prophets should speak, and the others discern.
text.DRB: And let Prophets speak two or three, and let the rest judge.

text.NRSV: If a revelation is made to someone else sitting nearby, let the first person be silent.
text.Rotherham: If, however, unto another, a revelation be made, as he is sitting, let the first be silent;
text.KJV: If any thing be revealed to another that sitteth by, let the first hold his peace.
text.NABRE: But if a revelation is given to another person sitting there, the first one should be silent.
text.DRB: But if it be revealed to another sitting, let the first hold his peace.

text.NRSV: For you can all prophesy one by one, so that all may learn and all be encouraged.
text.Rotherham: For, one by one, ye can, all, be prophesying,—that, all, may learn, and, all, be encouraged.
text.KJV: For ye may all prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be comforted.
text.NABRE: For you can all prophesy one by one, so that all may learn and all be encouraged.
text.DRB: For you may all prophesy one by one: that all may learn, and all may be exhorted:

text.NRSV: And the spirits of prophets are subject to the prophets,
text.Rotherham: And, spirits of prophets, unto prophets, do submit themselves;
text.KJV: And the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets.
text.NABRE: Indeed, the spirits of prophets are under the prophets’ control,
text.DRB: and the spirits of prophets are subject to prophets.

text.NRSV: for God is a God not of disorder but of peace. (As in all the churches of the saints,
text.Rotherham: For God is not [a God] of confusion, but, of peace:—as in all the assemblies of the saints.
text.KJV: For God is not the author of confusion, but of peace, as in all churches of the saints.
text.NABRE: since he is not the God of disorder but of peace. As in all the churches of the holy ones,
text.DRB: For God is not the God of dissension, but of peace: as also in all the Churches of the saints I teach.

text.NRSV: women should be silent in the churches. For they are not permitted to speak, but should be subordinate, as the law also says.
text.Rotherham: As for the women, in the assemblies, let them be silent, for it is not permitted them to be speaking; but let them be in submission,—even as, the law, saith.
text.KJV: Let your women keep silence in the churches: for it is not permitted unto them to speak; but they are commanded to be under obedience, as also saith the law.
text.NABRE: women should keep silent in the churches, for they are not allowed to speak, but should be subordinate, as even the law says.
text.DRB: Let women hold their peace in the Churches: for it is not permitted them to speak, but to be subject, as also the Law saith.

text.NRSV: If there is anything they desire to know, let them ask their husbands at home. For it is shameful for a woman to speak in church.
text.Rotherham: If, however, they are wishing to learn something, at home, their own husbands, let them question; for it is a shame for a woman to be speaking in assembly.
text.KJV: And if they will learn any thing, let them ask their husbands at home: for it is a shame for women to speak in the church.
text.NABRE: But if they want to learn anything, they should ask their husbands at home. For it is improper for a woman to speak in the church.
text.DRB: But if they list learn any thing, let them ask their own husbands at home. For it is a foul thing for a woman to speak in the Church.

text.NRSV: Or did the word of God originate with you? Or are you the only ones it has reached?)
text.Rotherham: Or, from you, did the word of God come forth? Or, unto you alone, did it extend?
text.KJV: What? came the word of God out from you? or came it unto you only?
text.NABRE: Did the word of God go forth from you? Or has it come to you alone?
text.DRB: Or did the word of God proceed from you? came it unto you only?

text.NRSV: Anyone who claims to be a prophet, or to have spiritual powers, must acknowledge that what I am writing to you is a command of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: If anyone thinketh himself to be a prophet, or spiritually gifted, let him acknowledge the things which I am writing to you,—that they are, a commandment, of the Lord.
text.KJV: If any man think himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him acknowledge that the things that I write unto you are the commandments of the Lord.
text.NABRE: If anyone thinks that he is a prophet or a spiritual person, he should recognize that what I am writing to you is a commandment of the Lord.
text.DRB: If any man seem to be a Prophet, or spiritual, let him know the things that I write to you, that they are the commandments of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Anyone who does not recognize this is not to be recognized.
text.Rotherham: But, if anyone knoweth not, he is unknown!
text.KJV: But if any man be ignorant, let him be ignorant.
text.NABRE: If anyone does not acknowledge this, he is not acknowledged.
text.DRB: But if any man know not, he shall not be known.

text.NRSV: So, my friends, be eager to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues;
text.Rotherham: So, then, my brethren,—be zealous to prophesy, and do not forbid, to be speaking with tongues;
text.KJV: Wherefore, brethren, covet to prophesy, and forbid not to speak with tongues.
text.NABRE: So, [my] brothers, strive eagerly to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues,
text.DRB: Therefore, brethren, be earnest to prophesy: and to speak with tongues prohibit not.

text.NRSV: but all things should be done decently and in order.
text.Rotherham: But let, all things, with comeliness, and by arrangement, be done.
text.KJV: Let all things be done decently and in order.
text.NABRE: but everything must be done properly and in order.
text.DRB: But let all things be done honestly and according to order among you.

text.NRSV: Now I would remind you, brothers and sisters, of the good news that I proclaimed to you, which you in turn received, in which also you stand,
text.Rotherham: Moreover, brethren, I make known unto you The joyful message, which I myself announced to you, which also ye received, in which also ye stand;
text.KJV: Moreover, brethren, I declare unto you the gospel which I preached unto you, which also ye have received, and wherein ye stand;
text.NABRE: V. The Resurrection A. The Resurrection of Christ Chapter 15 The Gospel Teaching. Now I am reminding you, brothers, of the gospel I preached to you, which you indeed received and in which you also stand.
text.DRB: And I do you to understand, brethren, the Gospel which I preached to you, which also you received, in the which also you stand,

text.NRSV: through which also you are being saved, if you hold firmly to the message that I proclaimed to you—unless you have come to believe in vain.
text.Rotherham: Through which also ye are being saved,—if ye hold fast, with what discourse, I announced the joyful message unto you; unless indeed, in vain, ye believed.
text.KJV: By which also ye are saved, if ye keep in memory what I preached unto you, unless ye have believed in vain.
text.NABRE: Through it you are also being saved, if you hold fast to the word I preached to you, unless you believed in vain.
text.DRB: by the which also you are saved, after what manner I preached unto you if you keep it, unless you have believed in vain.

text.NRSV: For I handed on to you as of first importance what I in turn had received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the scriptures,
text.Rotherham: For I delivered unto you, among the first things, what also I received:—how that Christ died for our sins, according to the Scriptures,
text.KJV: For I delivered unto you first of all that which I also received, how that Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures;
text.NABRE: For I handed on to you as of first importance what I also received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the scriptures;
text.DRB: For I delivered unto you first of all which I also received: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures:

text.NRSV: and that he was buried, and that he was raised on the third day in accordance with the scriptures,
text.Rotherham: And that he was buried, and that he hath been raised, on the third day, according to the Scriptures,—
text.KJV: And that he was buried, and that he rose again the third day according to the scriptures:
text.NABRE: that he was buried; that he was raised on the third day in accordance with the scriptures;
text.DRB: and that he was buried, and that he rose again the third day, according to the Scriptures:

text.NRSV: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
text.Rotherham: And that he appeared unto Cephas, then, to the twelve,
text.KJV: And that he was seen of Cephas, then of the twelve:
text.NABRE: that he appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
text.DRB: and that he was seen of Cephas; and after that of the eleven.

text.NRSV: Then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have died.
text.Rotherham: After that, he appeared to above five hundred brethren at once,—of whom, the greater number, remain until even now, but, some, have fallen asleep,—
text.KJV: After that, he was seen of above five hundred brethren at once; of whom the greater part remain unto this present, but some are fallen asleep.
text.NABRE: After that, he appeared to more than five hundred brothers at once, most of whom are still living, though some have fallen asleep.
text.DRB: Then was he seen of more than five hundred brethren together: of which many remain until this present, and some are a-sleep.

text.NRSV: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
text.Rotherham: After that, he appeared unto James, then, unto all the apostles,
text.KJV: After that, he was seen of James; then of all the apostles.
text.NABRE: After that he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
text.DRB: Moreover he was seen of James, then of all the Apostles.

text.NRSV: Last of all, as to one untimely born, he appeared also to me.
text.Rotherham: And, last of all, just as if unto the unseasonable birth, he appeared, even unto me;
text.KJV: And last of all he was seen of me also, as of one born out of due time.
text.NABRE: Last of all, as to one born abnormally, he appeared to me.
text.DRB: And last of all, as it were of an abortive he was seen also of me.

text.NRSV: For I am the least of the apostles, unfit to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
text.Rotherham: For, I, am the least of the apostles, who am not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the assembly of God.
text.KJV: For I am the least of the apostles, that am not meet to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
text.NABRE: For I am the least of the apostles, not fit to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
text.DRB: For I am the least of the Apostles, who am not worthy to be called an Apostle, because I persecuted the Church of God.

text.NRSV: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace toward me has not been in vain. On the contrary, I worked harder than any of them—though it was not I, but the grace of God that is with me.
text.Rotherham: But, by favour of God, I am what I am, and, his favour, which was unto me, hath not been made void,—but, much more abundantly than they all, have I toiled, albeit not, I, but the favour of God with me.
text.KJV: But by the grace of God I am what I am: and his grace which was bestowed upon me was not in vain; but I laboured more abundantly than they all: yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.
text.NABRE: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace to me has not been ineffective. Indeed, I have toiled harder than all of them; not I, however, but the grace of God [that is] with me.
text.DRB: But by the grace of God I am that which I am; & his grace in me hath not been void, but I have laboured more aboundantly than all they: yet not I, but the grace of God with me.

text.NRSV: Whether then it was I or they, so we proclaim and so you have come to believe.
text.Rotherham: Whether therefore, I, or, they, thus do we proclaim, and, thus did ye believe.
text.KJV: Therefore whether it were I or they, so we preach, and so ye believed.
text.NABRE: Therefore, whether it be I or they, so we preach and so you believed. B. The Resurrection of the Dead Results of Denial.
text.DRB: For whether I, or they, so we preach, and so you have believed.

text.NRSV: Now if Christ is proclaimed as raised from the dead, how can some of you say there is no resurrection of the dead?
text.Rotherham: Now, if, Christ is proclaimed, that, from among the dead, he hath been raised, how say some, among you—resurrection of the dead, there is none?
text.KJV: Now if Christ be preached that he rose from the dead, how say some among you that there is no resurrection of the dead?
text.NABRE: But if Christ is preached as raised from the dead, how can some among you say there is no resurrection of the dead?
text.DRB: But if Christ be preached that he is risen again from the dead, how do certain among you say, that there is no resurrection of the dead?

text.NRSV: If there is no resurrection of the dead, then Christ has not been raised;
text.Rotherham: But, if, resurrection of the dead, there is none, not even Christ, hath been raised;
text.KJV: But if there be no resurrection of the dead, then is Christ not risen:
text.NABRE: If there is no resurrection of the dead, then neither has Christ been raised.
text.DRB: And if there be no resurrection of the dead, neither is Christ risen again.

text.NRSV: and if Christ has not been raised, then our proclamation has been in vain and your faith has been in vain.
text.Rotherham: And, if Christ, hath not been raised, void, after all, is our proclamation, void also, our faith,—
text.KJV: And if Christ be not risen, then is our preaching vain, and your faith is also vain.
text.NABRE: And if Christ has not been raised, then empty [too] is our preaching; empty, too, your faith.
text.DRB: And if Christ be not risen again, then vain is our preaching, vain also is your faith.

text.NRSV: We are even found to be misrepresenting God, because we testified of God that he raised Christ—whom he did not raise if it is true that the dead are not raised.
text.Rotherham: And we are found, even false-witnesses of God, because we have witnessed respecting God, that he raised the Christ,—whom he did not raise, if, indeed, after all, the dead are not raised!
text.KJV: Yea, and we are found false witnesses of God; because we have testified of God that he raised up Christ: whom he raised not up, if so be that the dead rise not.
text.NABRE: Then we are also false witnesses to God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, whom he did not raise if in fact the dead are not raised.
text.DRB: And we are found also false witnesses of God: because we have given testimony against God, that he hath raised up Christ, whom he hath not raised up, if the dead rise not again.

text.NRSV: For if the dead are not raised, then Christ has not been raised.
text.Rotherham: For, if the dead are not raised, not even Christ, hath been raised;
text.KJV: For if the dead rise not, then is not Christ raised:
text.NABRE: For if the dead are not raised, neither has Christ been raised,
text.DRB: For if the dead rise not again, neither is Christ risen again.

text.NRSV: If Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile and you are still in your sins.
text.Rotherham: And, if Christ hath not been raised, to no purpose, is your faith, yet, are ye in your sins!
text.KJV: And if Christ be not raised, your faith is vain; ye are yet in your sins.
text.NABRE: and if Christ has not been raised, your faith is vain; you are still in your sins.
text.DRB: And if Christ be not risen again, vain is your faith, for yet you are in your sins.

text.NRSV: Then those also who have died in Christ have perished.
text.Rotherham: Hence also, they who are fallen asleep in Christ, are lost:
text.KJV: Then they also which are fallen asleep in Christ are perished.
text.NABRE: Then those who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
text.DRB: Then they also that are a-sleep in Christ, are perished.

text.NRSV: If for this life only we have hoped in Christ, we are of all people most to be pitied.
text.Rotherham: If, in this life, in Christ, we have hoped—and that is all, we are, of all men, most to be pitied.
text.KJV: If in this life only we have hope in Christ, we are of all men most miserable.
text.NABRE: If for this life only we have hoped in Christ, we are the most pitiable people of all. Christ the Firstfruits.
text.DRB: If in this life only we be hoping in Christ, we are more miserable than all men.

text.NRSV: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead, the first fruits of those who have died.
text.Rotherham: But, now, hath Christ been raised from among the dead,—a firstfruit of them who have fallen asleep;
text.KJV: But now is Christ risen from the dead, and become the firstfruits of them that slept.
text.NABRE: But now Christ has been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.
text.DRB: But now Christ is risen again from the dead, the first fruits of them that sleep:

text.NRSV: For since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead has also come through a human being;
text.Rotherham: For, since indeed, through a man, came death, through a man, also cometh the raising of the dead;
text.KJV: For since by man came death, by man came also the resurrection of the dead.
text.NABRE: For since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came also through a human being.
text.DRB: for by a man death; and by a man the resurrection of the dead.

text.NRSV: for as all die in Adam, so all will be made alive in Christ.
