text.Rotherham: For, just as, in the Adam, all die, so, also, in the Christ, shall all be made alive.
text.KJV: For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.
text.NABRE: For just as in Adam all die, so too in Christ shall all be brought to life,
text.DRB: And as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all shall be made alive.

text.NRSV: But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits, then at his coming those who belong to Christ.
text.Rotherham: But, each, in his own rank:—A firstfruit, Christ, after that, they who are the Christ’s, in his presence,
text.KJV: But every man in his own order: Christ the firstfruits; afterward they that are Christ’s at his coming.
text.NABRE: but each one in proper order: Christ the firstfruits; then, at his coming, those who belong to Christ;
text.DRB: But every one in his own order: the first fruits Christ, then they that are of Christ, that believed in his coming.

text.NRSV: Then comes the end, when he hands over the kingdom to God the Father, after he has destroyed every ruler and every authority and power.
text.Rotherham: Afterwards, the end—whensoever he delivereth up the kingdom unto his God and Father, whensoever he shall bring to nought all rule and all authority and power;
text.KJV: Then cometh the end, when he shall have delivered up the kingdom to God, even the Father; when he shall have put down all rule and all authority and power.
text.NABRE: then comes the end, when he hands over the kingdom to his God and Father, when he has destroyed every sovereignty and every authority and power.
text.DRB: Then the end, when he shall have delivered the Kingdom to God and the Father, when he shall have abolished all principality & authority & power.

text.NRSV: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
text.Rotherham: For he must needs reign, until he shall put all his enemies under his feet:
text.KJV: For he must reign, till he hath put all enemies under his feet.
text.NABRE: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
text.DRB: And he must reign, Until he put all his enemies under his feet.

text.NRSV: The last enemy to be destroyed is death.
text.Rotherham: As a last enemy, death, is to be destroyed;
text.KJV: The last enemy that shall be destroyed is death.
text.NABRE: The last enemy to be destroyed is death,
text.DRB: And the enemy death shall be destroyed last. For he hath subdued all things under his feet. And whereas he saith,

text.NRSV: For “God has put all things in subjection under his feet.” But when it says, “all things are put in subjection,” it is plain that this does not include the one who put all things in subjection under him.
text.Rotherham: For—He put, all things, in subjection under his feet. But, whensoever it shall be said—all things are in subjection!—it is evident that it means,—Except him who did put into subjection, unto him, the all things—
text.KJV: For he hath put all things under his feet. But when he saith all things are put under him, it is manifest that he is excepted, which did put all things under him.
text.NABRE: for “he subjected everything under his feet.” But when it says that everything has been subjected, it is clear that it excludes the one who subjected everything to him.
text.DRB: All things are subdued to him; undoubtedly, except him that subdued all things unto him.

text.NRSV: When all things are subjected to him, then the Son himself will also be subjected to the one who put all things in subjection under him, so that God may be all in all.
text.Rotherham: But whensoever have been put into subjection, unto him, the all things, then, the Son himself, [also] shall be put in subjection unto him who put in subjection, unto him, the all things,—that, God, may be, all things in all.
text.KJV: And when all things shall be subdued unto him, then shall the Son also himself be subject unto him that put all things under him, that God may be all in all.
text.NABRE: When everything is subjected to him, then the Son himself will [also] be subjected to the one who subjected everything to him, so that God may be all in all. Practical Arguments.
text.DRB: And when all things shall be subdued to him; then the Son also himself shall be subject to him that subdued all things unto him, that God may be all in all.

text.NRSV: Otherwise, what will those people do who receive baptism on behalf of the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why are people baptized on their behalf?
text.Rotherham: Else, what will they do, who are being immersed in behalf of the dead? If, not at all, are the dead to be raised, why are they even being immersed in their behalf?
text.KJV: Else what shall they do which are baptized for the dead, if the dead rise not at all? why are they then baptized for the dead?
text.NABRE: Otherwise, what will people accomplish by having themselves baptized for the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, then why are they having themselves baptized for them?
text.DRB: Otherwise what shall they do that are baptized for the dead, if the dead rise not again at all?

text.NRSV: And why are we putting ourselves in danger every hour?
text.Rotherham: Why also are, we, running into peril every hour?
text.KJV: And why stand we in jeopardy every hour?
text.NABRE: Moreover, why are we endangering ourselves all the time?
text.DRB: Why also are they baptized for them? Why also are we in danger every hour?

text.NRSV: I die every day! That is as certain, brothers and sisters, as my boasting of you—a boast that I make in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Day by day, am I dying!—Yea! by your own boasting, brethren, which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: I protest by your rejoicing which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.
text.NABRE: Every day I face death; I swear it by the pride in you [brothers] that I have in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.DRB: I die daily by your glory, brethren, which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: If with merely human hopes I fought with wild animals at Ephesus, what would I have gained by it? If the dead are not raised, “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
text.Rotherham: If, after the manner of men, I have fought with wild-beasts at Ephesus, what, to me, the profit? If the dead are not raised, Let us eat and drink, for, tomorrow, we die.
text.KJV: If after the manner of men I have fought with beasts at Ephesus, what advantageth it me, if the dead rise not? let us eat and drink; for to morrow we die.
text.NABRE: If at Ephesus I fought with beasts, so to speak, what benefit was it to me? If the dead are not raised: “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
text.DRB: If (according to man) I fought with beasts at Ephesus, what doth it profit me, if the dead rise not again? Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we shall die.

text.NRSV: Do not be deceived: “Bad company ruins good morals.”
text.Rotherham: Be not deceiving yourselves,—evil communications corrupt gentle manners:—
text.KJV: Be not deceived: evil communications corrupt good manners.
text.NABRE: Do not be led astray: “Bad company corrupts good morals.”
text.DRB: Be not seduced, Evil communications corrupt good manners.

text.NRSV: Come to a sober and right mind, and sin no more; for some people have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.
text.Rotherham: Wake up to sobriety, in righteousness, and be not committing sin; for some have, an ignorance of God: for shame, unto you, am I speaking!
text.KJV: Awake to righteousness, and sin not; for some have not the knowledge of God: I speak this to your shame.
text.NABRE: Become sober as you ought and stop sinning. For some have no knowledge of God; I say this to your shame.
text.DRB: Awake ye just, and sin not. For some have not the knowledge of God, I speak to your shame.

text.NRSV: But someone will ask, “How are the dead raised? With what kind of body do they come?”
text.Rotherham: But some one will say—How, are the dead raised? and, with what kind of body, do they come?
text.KJV: But some man will say, How are the dead raised up? and with what body do they come?
text.NABRE: C. The Manner of the Resurrection But someone may say, “How are the dead raised? With what kind of body will they come back?”
text.DRB: But some man saith: How do the dead rise again? and with what manner of body shall they come?

text.NRSV: Fool! What you sow does not come to life unless it dies.
text.Rotherham: Simple one! What, thou, sowest, is not quickened, except it die;
text.KJV: Thou fool, that which thou sowest is not quickened, except it die:
text.NABRE: The Resurrection Body. You fool! What you sow is not brought to life unless it dies.
text.DRB: Fool, that which thou sowest is not quickened, unless it die first.

text.NRSV: And as for what you sow, you do not sow the body that is to be, but a bare seed, perhaps of wheat or of some other grain.
text.Rotherham: And, what thou sowest, not the body that shall come into existence, dost thou sow, but a naked kernel—if it so happen, of wheat, or of any of the rest,—
text.KJV: And that which thou sowest, thou sowest not that body that shall be, but bare grain, it may chance of wheat, or of some other grain:
text.NABRE: And what you sow is not the body that is to be but a bare kernel of wheat, perhaps, or of some other kind;
text.DRB: And that which thou sowest, not the body that shall be, doest thou sow; but bare grain, to wit, of wheat, or of some of the rest.

text.NRSV: But God gives it a body as he has chosen, and to each kind of seed its own body.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, God, giveth it a body, as he pleased, and, unto each of the seeds, a body of its own.
text.KJV: But God giveth it a body as it hath pleased him, and to every seed his own body.
text.NABRE: but God gives it a body as he chooses, and to each of the seeds its own body.
text.DRB: And God giveth it a body as he will: & to every seed his proper body.

text.NRSV: Not all flesh is alike, but there is one flesh for human beings, another for animals, another for birds, and another for fish.
text.Rotherham: Not all flesh, is the same flesh; but, one, indeed, is, [the flesh] of men, and, another, the flesh of beasts, and, another, the flesh of birds, and, another, of fishes;
text.KJV: All flesh is not the same flesh: but there is one kind of flesh of men, another flesh of beasts, another of fishes, and another of birds.
text.NABRE: Not all flesh is the same, but there is one kind for human beings, another kind of flesh for animals, another kind of flesh for birds, and another for fish.
text.DRB: Not all flesh, is the same flesh: but one of men, another of beasts, another of birds, another of fishes.

text.NRSV: There are terrestrial bodies and there are celestial bodies, but the glory of the celestial is one, and the glory of the terrestrial is another.
text.Rotherham: And there are heavenly bodies, and earthly bodies,—but, of one kind, indeed, is the glory of the heavenly, and, of another kind, is the glory of the earthly;—
text.KJV: There are also celestial bodies, and bodies terrestrial: but the glory of the celestial is one, and the glory of the terrestrial is another.
text.NABRE: There are both heavenly bodies and earthly bodies, but the brightness of the heavenly is one kind and that of the earthly another.
text.DRB: And bodies celestial, & bodies terrestrial: but, one glory of the celestial, and another of the terrestrial.

text.NRSV: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; indeed, star differs from star in glory.
text.Rotherham: One, is the glory of a sun, and, another, the glory of a moon, and, another, the glory of stars,—nay! star from star, differeth in glory.
text.KJV: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars: for one star differeth from another star in glory.
text.NABRE: The brightness of the sun is one kind, the brightness of the moon another, and the brightness of the stars another. For star differs from star in brightness.
text.DRB: One glory of the sun, another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars. For star differeth from star in glory:

text.NRSV: So it is with the resurrection of the dead. What is sown is perishable, what is raised is imperishable.
text.Rotherham: Thus, also the resurrection of the dead: it is sown in corruption, it is raised in incorruption,
text.KJV: So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption:
text.NABRE: So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown corruptible; it is raised incorruptible.
text.DRB: so also the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption, it shall rise in incorruption.

text.NRSV: It is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness, it is raised in power.
text.Rotherham: It is sown in dishonour, it is raised in glory, it is sown in weakness, it is raised in power,
text.KJV: It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in glory: it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:
text.NABRE: It is sown dishonorable; it is raised glorious. It is sown weak; it is raised powerful.
text.DRB: It is sown in dishonour, it shall rise in glory. It is sown in infirmity, it shall rise in power.

text.NRSV: It is sown a physical body, it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a physical body, there is also a spiritual body.
text.Rotherham: It is sown a body of the soul, it is raised a body of the spirit; if there is a body of the soul, there is also of the spirit:—
text.KJV: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body, and there is a spiritual body.
text.NABRE: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual one.
text.DRB: It is sown a natural body; it shall rise a spiritual body. If there be a natural body, there is also a spiritual,

text.NRSV: Thus it is written, “The first man, Adam, became a living being”; the last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
text.Rotherham: Thus, also, it is written—The first man, Adam, became, a living soul, the last Adam, a life-giving spirit.
text.KJV: And so it is written, The first man Adam was made a living soul; the last Adam was made a quickening spirit.
text.NABRE: So, too, it is written, “The first man, Adam, became a living being,” the last Adam a life-giving spirit.
text.DRB: as it is written: The first man Adam was made into a living soul: the last Adam into a quickening spirit.

text.NRSV: But it is not the spiritual that is first, but the physical, and then the spiritual.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, not first, is the [body] of the spirit, but that, of the soul,—afterwards, that of the spirit.
text.KJV: Howbeit that was not first which is spiritual, but that which is natural; and afterward that which is spiritual.
text.NABRE: But the spiritual was not first; rather the natural and then the spiritual.
text.DRB: Yet that is not first which is spiritual, but that which is natural: afterward that which is spiritual.

text.NRSV: The first man was from the earth, a man of dust; the second man is from heaven.
text.Rotherham: The first man, is of the ground, earthy, the second man, is, of heaven:
text.KJV: The first man is of the earth, earthy: the second man is the Lord from heaven.
text.NABRE: The first man was from the earth, earthly; the second man, from heaven.
text.DRB: The first man of earth, earthly: the second man from Heaven, heavenly.

text.NRSV: As was the man of dust, so are those who are of the dust; and as is the man of heaven, so are those who are of heaven.
text.Rotherham: As, the man of earth, such, also, the men of earth, and, as, the man of heaven, such, also, the men of heaven;
text.KJV: As is the earthy, such are they also that are earthy: and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.
text.NABRE: As was the earthly one, so also are the earthly, and as is the heavenly one, so also are the heavenly.
text.DRB: Such as is the earthly, such also are the earthly: and such as the heavenly, such also are the heavenly.

text.NRSV: Just as we have borne the image of the man of dust, we will also bear the image of the man of heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, even as we have borne the image of the man of earth, let us also bear the image of the man of heaven.
text.KJV: And as we have borne the image of the earthy, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly.
text.NABRE: Just as we have borne the image of the earthly one, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly one.
text.DRB: Therefore as we have borne the image of the earthly, let us bear also the image of the heavenly.

text.NRSV: What I am saying, brothers and sisters, is this: flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.
text.Rotherham: And, this, I say, brethren,—that, flesh and blood, cannot inherit, God’s kingdom. Neither doth, corruption, inherit, incorruption.
text.KJV: Now this I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God; neither doth corruption inherit incorruption.
text.NABRE: The Resurrection Event. This I declare, brothers: flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does corruption inherit incorruption.
text.DRB: This I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot possess the Kingdom of God: neither shall corruption possess incorruption.

text.NRSV: Listen, I will tell you a mystery! We will not all die, but we will all be changed,
text.Rotherham: Lo! a sacred secret, unto you, do I declare:—we shall not, all, sleep, but we shall, all, be changed,—
text.KJV: Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,
text.NABRE: Behold, I tell you a mystery. We shall not all fall asleep, but we will all be changed,
text.DRB: Behold I tell you a mystery. We shall all indeed rise again: but we shall not all be changed.

text.NRSV: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised imperishable, and we will be changed.
text.Rotherham: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, during the last trumpet; for it shall sound, and, the dead, shall be raised, incorruptible, and, we, shall be changed.
text.KJV: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump: for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.
text.NABRE: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.
text.DRB: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet (for the trumpet shall sound) and the dead shall rise again incorruptible: and we shall be changed.

text.NRSV: For this perishable body must put on imperishability, and this mortal body must put on immortality.
text.Rotherham: For this corruptible must needs clothe itself with incorruptibility, and this mortal, clothe itself, with immortality.
text.KJV: For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.
text.NABRE: For that which is corruptible must clothe itself with incorruptibility, and that which is mortal must clothe itself with immortality.
text.DRB: For this corruptible must do on incorruption; & this mortal do on immortality.

text.NRSV: When this perishable body puts on imperishability, and this mortal body puts on immortality, then the saying that is written will be fulfilled: “Death has been swallowed up in victory.”
text.Rotherham: But, whensoever, this mortal, shall clothe itself with immortality, then, shall be brought to pass the saying that is written—Death hath been swallowed up, victoriously;
text.KJV: So when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.
text.NABRE: And when this which is corruptible clothes itself with incorruptibility and this which is mortal clothes itself with immortality, then the word that is written shall come about: “Death is swallowed up in victory.
text.DRB: And when this mortal hath done on immortality, then shall come to pass the saying that is written: Death is swallowed up in victory.

text.NRSV: “Where, O death, is your victory? Where, O death, is your sting?”
text.Rotherham: Where, O death, is thy victory? Where, O death, is thy sting?
text.KJV: O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory?
text.NABRE: Where, O death, is your victory? Where, O death, is your sting?”
text.DRB: Death where is thy victory? Death where is thy sting?

text.NRSV: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
text.Rotherham: Now, the sting of death, is, sin, and, the power of sin, is, the law;—
text.KJV: The sting of death is sin; and the strength of sin is the law.
text.NABRE: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
text.DRB: And the sting of death, is sin: and the power of sin is the Law.

text.NRSV: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: But, unto God, be thanks, who is giving unto us the victory, through our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: But thanks be to God, which giveth us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: But thanks be to God who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: But thanks be to God that hath given us the victory by our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Therefore, my beloved, be steadfast, immovable, always excelling in the work of the Lord, because you know that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.
text.Rotherham: So, then, my beloved brethren,—become ye, steadfast, immovable, superabounding in the work of the Lord, at all times; knowing that, your toil, is not in vain in the Lord.
text.KJV: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye stedfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.
text.NABRE: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be firm, steadfast, always fully devoted to the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.
text.DRB: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be stable and unmoveable; abounding in the work of our Lord always, knowing that your labour is not vain in our Lord:

text.NRSV: Now concerning the collection for the saints: you should follow the directions I gave to the churches of Galatia.
text.Rotherham: Now, concerning the collection which is for the saints, just as I directed the assemblies of Galatia, so, also do, ye:—
text.KJV: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I have given order to the churches of Galatia, even so do ye.
text.NABRE: VI. Conclusion Chapter 16 The Collection. Now in regard to the collection for the holy ones, you also should do as I ordered the churches of Galatia.
text.DRB: And concerning the collections that are made for the Saints, as I have ordained to the Churches of Galatia, so do ye also.

text.NRSV: On the first day of every week, each of you is to put aside and save whatever extra you earn, so that collections need not be taken when I come.
text.Rotherham: Upon the first of the week, let, each one of you, put, by itself, in store, as he may be prospering,—lest, as soon as I come, then, collections, should be in progress.
text.KJV: Upon the first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as God hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come.
text.NABRE: On the first day of the week each of you should set aside and save whatever he can afford, so that collections will not be going on when I come.
text.DRB: In the first of the Sabboth let every one of you put a part with himself, laying up what shall well like him: that not when I come, then collections be made.

text.NRSV: And when I arrive, I will send any whom you approve with letters to take your gift to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, as soon as I arrive, whomsoever ye shall approve by letters, these, will I send, to bear away your favour unto Jerusalem:
text.KJV: And when I come, whomsoever ye shall approve by your letters, them will I send to bring your liberality unto Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: And when I arrive, I shall send those whom you have approved with letters of recommendation to take your gracious gift to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And when I shall be present; whom you shall approve by letters, them will I send to carry your grace into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: If it seems advisable that I should go also, they will accompany me.
text.Rotherham: And, if it be meet that, I also, be journeying, with me, shall they journey.
text.KJV: And if it be meet that I go also, they shall go with me.
text.NABRE: If it seems fitting that I should go also, they will go with me. Paul’s Travel Plans.
text.DRB: And if it be worthy that I also go, they shall go with me.

text.NRSV: I will visit you after passing through Macedonia—for I intend to pass through Macedonia—
text.Rotherham: Now I will come unto you, as soon as I have passed through Macedonia,—for I do pass through Macedonia,
text.KJV: Now I will come unto you, when I shall pass through Macedonia: for I do pass through Macedonia.
text.NABRE: I shall come to you after I pass through Macedonia (for I am going to pass through Macedonia),
text.DRB: And I will come to you, when I shall have passed through Macedonia. For I will pass through Macedonia.

text.NRSV: and perhaps I will stay with you or even spend the winter, so that you may send me on my way, wherever I go.
text.Rotherham: And, with you, I may perhaps sojourn, or winter, that, ye, may set me forward, whithersoever I may be journeying.
text.KJV: And it may be that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever I go.
text.NABRE: and perhaps I shall stay or even spend the winter with you, so that you may send me on my way wherever I may go.
text.DRB: And with you perhaps I will abide, or will winter also: that you may bring me on my way whithersoever I go.

text.NRSV: I do not want to see you now just in passing, for I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord permits.
text.Rotherham: For I do not wish to see you, just now, by the way; for I hope to remain, some time, with you,—if, the Lord, permit.
text.KJV: For I will not see you now by the way; but I trust to tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.
text.NABRE: For I do not wish to see you now just in passing, but I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord permits.
text.DRB: For I will not now see you by the way, for I hope that I shall abide with you some little time, if our Lord will permit.

text.NRSV: But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
text.Rotherham: But I remain in Ephesus, until the Pentecost,
text.KJV: But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.
text.NABRE: I shall stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
text.DRB: But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.

text.NRSV: for a wide door for effective work has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
text.Rotherham: For, a door, unto me, hath opened, great and effectual, and, opposers, are many.
text.KJV: For a great door and effectual is opened unto me, and there are many adversaries.
text.NABRE: because a door has opened for me wide and productive for work, but there are many opponents.
text.DRB: For a great door and evident is opened unto me: and many adversaries.

text.NRSV: If Timothy comes, see that he has nothing to fear among you, for he is doing the work of the Lord just as I am;
text.Rotherham: But, if Timothy should come, see that, without fear, he be with you, for, in the work of the Lord, doth he labour, even as, I:
text.KJV: Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also do.
text.NABRE: If Timothy comes, see that he is without fear in your company, for he is doing the work of the Lord just as I am.
text.DRB: And if Timothee come, see that he be without fear with you, for he worketh the work of our Lord, as also I.

text.NRSV: therefore let no one despise him. Send him on his way in peace, so that he may come to me; for I am expecting him with the brothers.
text.Rotherham: Let no one then despise him; and set ye him forward in peace, that he may come unto me, for I expect him with the brethren.
text.KJV: Let no man therefore despise him: but conduct him forth in peace, that he may come unto me: for I look for him with the brethren.
text.NABRE: Therefore, no one should disdain him. Rather, send him on his way in peace that he may come to me, for I am expecting him with the brothers.
text.DRB: Let no man therefore despise him, but conduct ye him in peace: that he may come to me. For I expect him with the brethren.

text.NRSV: Now concerning our brother Apollos, I strongly urged him to visit you with the other brothers, but he was not at all willing to come now. He will come when he has the opportunity.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning Apollos the brother, much, did I beseech him, that he would come unto you, with the brethren, but there was, by no means, any will, that he should come, now;—he will come, however, as soon as he hath good opportunity.
text.KJV: As touching our brother Apollos, I greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren: but his will was not at all to come at this time; but he will come when he shall have convenient time.
text.NABRE: Now in regard to our brother Apollos, I urged him strongly to go to you with the brothers, but it was not at all his will that he go now. He will go when he has an opportunity.
text.DRB: And of brother Apollo I do you to understand, that I much entreated him to come unto you with the brethren: and at all it was not his mind to come now. But he will come when he shall have leisure.

text.NRSV: Keep alert, stand firm in your faith, be courageous, be strong.
text.Rotherham: Be on the watch, stand firm in the faith, be men—be strong;
text.KJV: Watch ye, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men, be strong.
text.NABRE: Exhortation and Greetings. Be on your guard, stand firm in the faith, be courageous, be strong.
text.DRB: Watch ye, stand in the faith, do manfully, & be strengthened.

text.NRSV: Let all that you do be done in love.
text.Rotherham: Let, all your affairs, in love, be carried on.
text.KJV: Let all your things be done with charity.
text.NABRE: Your every act should be done with love.
text.DRB: Let all your things be done in charity.

text.NRSV: Now, brothers and sisters, you know that members of the household of Stephanas were the first converts in Achaia, and they have devoted themselves to the service of the saints;
text.Rotherham: Now I beseech you, brethren,—ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is a first-fruit of Achaia, and, for the purpose of ministering, they devoted themselves, unto the saints—
text.KJV: I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints,)
text.NABRE: I urge you, brothers—you know that the household of Stephanas is the firstfruits of Achaia and that they have devoted themselves to the service of the holy ones—
text.DRB: And I beseech you, brethren, you know the house of Stephanas, and of Fortunatus, that they are the first fruits of Achaia, & have ordained themselves to the ministry of the saints:

text.NRSV: I urge you to put yourselves at the service of such people, and of everyone who works and labors with them.
text.Rotherham: That, ye also, be submitting yourselves unto such as these,—and unto everyone helping in the work and toiling.
text.KJV: That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with us, and laboureth.
text.NABRE: be subordinate to such people and to everyone who works and toils with them.
text.DRB: that you also be subject to such, and to every one that helpeth & laboureth with us.

text.NRSV: I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because they have made up for your absence;
text.Rotherham: I Rejoice, moreover, in the presence of Stephanas, and Fortunatus, and Achaicus: because, your own shortcoming, these, have filled up:—
text.KJV: I am glad of the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was lacking on your part they have supplied.
text.NABRE: I rejoice in the arrival of Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus, because they made up for your absence,
text.DRB: And I rejoice in the presence of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because that which you wanted, they have supplied.

text.NRSV: for they refreshed my spirit as well as yours. So give recognition to such persons.
text.Rotherham: They have given rest, in fact, unto my spirit, and yours: hold in acknowledgment, therefore, such as these.
text.KJV: For they have refreshed my spirit and yours: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.
text.NABRE: for they refreshed my spirit as well as yours. So give recognition to such people.
text.DRB: For they have refreshed both my spirit and yours. Know them therefore that are such.

text.NRSV: The churches of Asia send greetings. Aquila and Prisca, together with the church in their house, send greetings in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: The assemblies of Asia salute you: Aquila and Priscilla, with the assembly meeting at their house, salute you much in the Lord:
text.KJV: The churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.
text.NABRE: The churches of Asia send you greetings. Aquila and Prisca together with the church at their house send you many greetings in the Lord.
text.DRB: The Churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla with their domestical Church salute you much in our Lord.

text.NRSV: All the brothers and sisters send greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
text.Rotherham: All the brethren salute you:—Salute ye one another with a holy kiss.
text.KJV: All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy kiss.
text.NABRE: All the brothers greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
text.DRB: All the brethren salute you. Salute one another in a holy kiss.

text.NRSV: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand.
text.Rotherham: The salutation of Paul—with my own hand.
text.KJV: The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand.
text.NABRE: I, Paul, write you this greeting in my own hand.
text.DRB: The salutation with mine own hand Paul's.

text.NRSV: Let anyone be accursed who has no love for the Lord. Our Lord, come!
text.Rotherham: If anyone doth not dearly love the Lord, let him be anathema [that is, "accursed"]: Maran atha [that is, "The Lord, cometh"].
text.KJV: If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maran-atha.
text.NABRE: If anyone does not love the Lord, let him be accursed. Marana tha.
text.DRB: If any man love not our Lord Jesus Christ, be he anathema. Maran atha.

text.NRSV: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
text.Rotherham: The favour of the Lord Jesus, be with you.
text.KJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
text.NABRE: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
text.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

text.NRSV: My love be with all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.
text.Rotherham: My love, be with you all, in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen. The first epistle to the Corinthians was written from Philippi by Stephanas, and Fortunatus, and Achaicus, and Timotheus.
text.NABRE: My love to all of you in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: My charity be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
